Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2) **lib/container/RemoteRuntimeModule.js:1** * The mapping exposure is conditional on `__webpack_require__.federation` already being initialized. If `RemoteRuntimeModule` runs before `InvalidateFederatedModuleRuntimeModule` installs the federation object (runtime module ordering can vary when multiple runtimeRequirements are processed), `_remoteMapping` will never be published and `invalidateRemote` won’t be able to reset `data.p`. Consider ensuring the namespace exists here before assignment (e.g. initialize `__webpack_require__.federation` to an object if missing) so `_remoteMapping` is always set deterministically. **lib/container/InvalidateFederatedModuleRuntimeModule.js:1** * This runtime module unconditionally overwrites `__webpack_require__.federation` with a fresh object. That makes the runtime fragile if other runtime modules (now or in the future) attach additional properties to `__webpack_require__.federation`, and it also interacts poorly with ordering (it can clobber a previously attached mapping/utilities). Prefer extending an existing object (`__webpack_require__.federation ||= {}`-style) and only initializing missing fields, so multiple runtime modules can safely contribute to the namespace.

body: `__webpack_federation__` is now always available as a parsed magic identifier, but the runtime provider for `RuntimeGlobals.federation` is only registered via `ContainerReferencePlugin` (i.e. when remotes are configured). This means builds that reference `__webpack_federation__` without MF remotes can compile yet have `__webpack_require__.federation` undefined at runtime. Consider either (a) providing a minimal federation runtime module whenever `RuntimeGlobals.federation` is required (even without remotes), or (b) emitting a compile-time error when `__webpack_federation__` is used but no federation runtime can be installed, to avoid a confusing runtime failure mode.

body: ## Pull request overview Adds a new GitHub Actions workflow to run the external `peakoss/anti-slop` action on PRs, aiming to automatically detect low-quality / non-compliant PR submissions using heuristic checks rather than an LLM. **Changes:** - Introduces a new “PR Quality” workflow that runs `peakoss/anti-slop` on `pull_request_target`. - Configures the action to require a PR template and cap failures via `max-failures`. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
`pull_request_target` is currently limited to `types: [opened, reopened]`, so this job will not re-run when new commits are pushed (`synchronize`) or when the PR description/template is updated (`edited`). That can leave the check in a failing state until someone manually re-runs it. Consider adding at least `synchronize` and `edited` (and optionally `ready_for_review`) to the event types.
```suggestion
    types: [opened, reopened, synchronize, edited, ready_for_review]
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Using `pull_request_target` together with `pull-requests: write` gives this workflow a write-capable token on untrusted PRs (including forks). Even without a checkout step, this expands blast radius if the third-party action ever becomes compromised. Consider minimizing permissions to only what the action needs (e.g. drop `contents`/`issues` if unused), and/or gating write permissions to same-repo PRs (pattern used elsewhere: `if: github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository`) while running a read-only variant for fork PRs.

body: ## Pull request overview This PR addresses resolution of `node:`-prefixed module requests (e.g. `node:fs`) so that `resolve.alias` / `resolve.fallback` can intercept them (notably allowing `alias: false` to ignore the module) instead of failing later with `UnhandledSchemeError`. **Changes:** - Update `NormalModuleFactory` to run the normal resolver for the `node` scheme and treat `resolvedResource === false` as an ignored module. - Add a new config case to verify `resolve.alias: { "node:fs": false }` ignores the request. - Add a changeset entry for a patch release. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/NormalModuleFactory.js` | Ensures `node:` scheme requests go through the resolver path so alias/fallback can return `false` and the module can be ignored. | | `test/configCases/resolve/node-prefix-alias/webpack.config.js` | Adds a config case that aliases `node:fs` to `false` under `target: "web"`. | | `test/configCases/resolve/node-prefix-alias/index.js` | Asserts that requiring `node:fs` yields an ignored module (empty exports) when aliased to `false`. | | `.changeset/fix-node-prefix-alias.md` | Declares a patch release note for the fix. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer
copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: resolveForScheme and resolveResource are started in parallel but both can influence the effective resource (resolveForScheme via mutating the resourceData object, resolveResource via reassigning resourceData). This introduces a race where scheme hook mutations can be lost or produce nondeterministic results if a plugin ever handles the "node" scheme. To avoid this, run resolveResource after resolveForScheme completes (or otherwise serialize/apply both results deterministically).

## Pull request overview This PR aims to switch the project’s type declaration generation from `webpack/tooling` to using TypeScript (`tsc`), updating references and docs to align with a `types/` output directory rather than a single `types.d.ts` file. **Changes:** - Updated package metadata and scripts to point TypeScript declarations to `types/index.d.ts` and add a `build:types`/publish-time generation step. - Rewired JSDoc type imports in benchmark code/configs to import types from the package root instead of `types.d.ts`. - Adjusted configs/ignores/docs to treat `types/` as generated output (gitignored, prettier/cspell ignored, docs updated). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 37 out of 39 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | tsconfig.validation.json | Validates generated declarations via `types/index.d.ts` | | tsconfig.types.json | Introduces a dedicated tsconfig intended for emitting type declarations | | package.json | Points `types` to `types/index.d.ts`, updates published files/scripts for type generation | | test/BenchmarkTestCases.benchmark.mjs | Switches type imports to package-root module typings | | test/benchmarkCases/**/webpack.config.mjs | Updates config typing to use package-root `Configuration` type | | lib/Dependency.js | Adds explicit `symbol` JSDoc casting for better TS inference/emit | | lib/dependencies/HarmonyImportSpecifierDependency.js | Adds explicit `symbol` JSDoc casting | | lib/dependencies/HarmonyExportImportedSpecifierDependency.js | Adds explicit `symbol` JSDoc casting | | lib/dependencies/CommonJsExportRequireDependency.js | Adds explicit `symbol` JSDoc casting | | cspell.json | Updates ignore from `types.d.ts` to `types/**` | | CONTRIBUTING.md | Updates contributor guidance to reference `types/` output | | AGENTS.md | Updates repo structure/workflow docs to reference `types/` | | .prettierignore | Ignores generated `types` output | | .gitignore | Ignores generated `/types` output |

author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `tsconfig.types.json` extends `tsconfig.json`, which sets `noEmit: true` (tsconfig.json:8). As a result, `yarn build:types` (`tsc -p tsconfig.types.json`) will not emit any `.d.ts` files, so `types/index.d.ts` won’t be produced. Additionally, `rootDir: "lib"` conflicts with the included `declarations.d.ts` / `declarations/*.d.ts` (outside `lib/`), which can trigger TS6059. Consider overriding `noEmit`, enabling declaration emit (e.g., `declaration` + `emitDeclarationOnly`), configuring an explicit output dir (e.g., `declarationDir`/`outDir` to `types/`), and adjusting/removing `rootDir` so included declaration files are compatible.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `package.json` now points `types` to `types/index.d.ts`, but the repo doesn’t contain `types/` (it’s gitignored) and the current `build:types` config (see `tsconfig.types.json` + base `noEmit`) won’t generate it. Also, emitted `.d.ts` files from `lib/**` are very likely to retain type references like `import("../declarations/WebpackOptions")` (e.g., `lib/index.js`), but `declarations/` and `declarations.d.ts` are not included in the published `files` list—this would make the published type declarations resolve to missing paths. Ensure the build generates `types/index.d.ts` and either bundles/copies the `declarations*` typings into `types/` or includes `declarations/` + `declarations.d.ts` in the published package.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Generating `types/` only in `prepublishOnly` won’t cover workflows that rely on `npm pack`/packing (which runs `prepack`, not `prepublishOnly`). Since `types/` is now ignored and expected to be generated, consider moving this to `prepack` (or adding `prepack` as well) so packed artifacts consistently include generated declarations.
```suggestion
    "prepare": "husky",
    "prepack": "yarn build:types",
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
In `.prettierignore`, the pattern `types` will match any path segment named `types`, not just the intended root `types/` output directory. To avoid accidentally skipping formatting in unrelated nested `types/` folders (if they ever appear), prefer a more specific ignore like `/types/` or `types/`.
```suggestion
/types/
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
`CONTRIBUTING.md` now says running `yarn fix` will regenerate type declarations into `types/`, but `yarn fix` currently runs `fix:code && fix:special && fmt` and does not call `build:types`. Given `types/` is gitignored and `AGENTS.md` says it’s generated at publish time, this instruction looks incorrect/out-of-date. Update the doc to reference the actual command developers should run locally to generate/validate types (e.g., `yarn build:types` or `yarn validate:types`), or adjust scripts so `yarn fix` produces `types/` as stated.
```suggestion
Then, automatically generate the type declarations by running `yarn build:types` locally, and the changes you have made will be reflected in the `types/` directory.
```

body: ## Pull request overview This PR tightens the repository’s PR quality gate by increasing the strictness of the `peakoss/anti-slop` GitHub Action configuration in the PR Quality workflow. **Changes:** - Enforce conventional PR titles. - Enforce conventional commit messages on PR commits. - Add contributor-behavior thresholds (daily forks limit, minimum global merge ratio) and lower the allowed failure count. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `require-conventional-title` / `require-conventional-commits` rely on PR title edits and new commits, but this workflow only runs on `pull_request_target` `opened`/`reopened`. That means title fixes (edited) and additional commits (synchronize) won’t be re-checked, and a PR could be incorrectly closed or miss new violations. Consider adding `edited` and `synchronize` (and possibly `ready_for_review`) to the trigger types so these new checks are enforced on updates.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: PR description says the PR will be closed if **any** existing check fails, or if **any three** of the new checks fail. With a single `max-failures: 3` threshold, it appears (depending on anti-slop’s semantics) that a PR could fail an existing check like `require-pr-template` but not be closed until 3 total failures. Please confirm how `max-failures` is applied, and adjust the config (or update the description) so the implemented behavior matches the intended policy.

body:
Calling `group.getChildren()` without any assertion doesn’t validate ChunkGroup integrity, and it won’t catch parent/child linkage issues (the method just returns a value). If the goal is integrity checking, use an invariant check (e.g. `group.checkConstraints()`) or assert expected parent/child relationships so failures are actionable.
```suggestion
						group.checkConstraints();
```

body: Yeah, it's one bug fix. When we enter parsing for an import/export statement of module, `prevStatement` will be assigned, but currently it's never cleared then. This causes incorrect behavior in anything that relies on `prevStatement` at `preWalk` stage.
author: hai-x

body: Yes, it's a one-line type fix. `_preWalkVariableDeclaration` should accept a parameter of type `VariableDeclaration`, so it will pass a `VariableDeclaration` as the statement argument to `preDeclarator`.

Webpack requires an entry point, so running this example via `webpack --config webpack.config.js` will fail unless callers always provide `--entry` on the CLI. Consider restoring `entry: './example.js'` (or setting a shared `entry` variable) and, if the README build pipeline needs CLI-provided entry/output, update `build.js` to set `global.NO_TARGET_ARGS = true` for this example to avoid overriding the per-compiler `output.filename` settings. ``` /** @type {import("webpack").Configuration[]} */ const config = [ // oxc { mode: "production", optimization: { chunkIds: "deterministic" // To keep filename consistent between different modes (for example building only) }, output: { filename: "oxc.[name].js" }, module: { ``` **examples/custom-javascript-parser/README.md:401** * The README’s embedded `Configuration example` now shows configs without an `entry`, which makes the example config incomplete unless users pass `--entry` on the CLI. Additionally, the `Info` section shows assets named `output.js`, which conflicts with the `output.filename` values shown in the config snippet, making the documentation internally inconsistent. Once the config/build setup is corrected, please regenerate this README section so the shown output matches the configuration. ``` /** @type {import("webpack").Configuration[]} */ const config = [ // oxc { mode: "production", optimization: { chunkIds: "deterministic" // To keep filename consistent between different modes (for example building only) }, output: { filename: "oxc.[name].js" }, module: { ```

body:
## Pull request overview This PR updates the Changesets GitHub changelog generator to harden link generation by validating commit hashes, repo slugs, and usernames before constructing GitHub links (intended to address a reported security finding in this file). **Changes:** - Added `isValidCommitHash`, `isValidRepoSlug`, and `isValidUsername` helpers and applied them before fetching/formatting GitHub links. - Adjusted dependency release line generation to build commit link lists more defensively. - Filtered usernames parsed from changeset summaries before turning them into profile links.
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **.changeset/changelog-generator.mjs:157** * After filtering `usersFromSummary` with `isValidUsername`, it’s possible for the filtered list to become empty; in that case `users` becomes an empty string and the code won’t fall back to `links.user`, so the generated suffix may lose author information. Consider falling back to `links.user` when the filtered/joined username list is empty. ``` const users = usersFromSummary.length ? usersFromSummary .filter(isValidUsername) .map( (userFromSummary) => `[@${userFromSummary}](${GITHUB_SERVER_URL}/${userFromSummary})` ) .join(", ") : links.user; ```
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The PR title/description and vulnerability section refer to SQL injection and “parameterized queries”, but this file contains no SQL/query execution logic (only GitHub link generation). The change here adds input validation and adjusts string construction, so either the PR description/rule metadata should be updated to match the actual issue being fixed, or the intended SQL-related fix is missing from this PR.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `options.repo` is now being used behind `isValidRepoSlug(options.repo)` guards, but an invalid `repo` value will currently just produce empty commit links (e.g., "Updated dependencies []") without surfacing a configuration error. Consider validating `options.repo` once up-front (after the existing `!options.repo` check) and throwing a clear error if it’s not a valid `owner/repo` slug, instead of silently skipping link generation.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Repo slug validation is applied in the commit-link paths, but in the `prFromSummary` branch `getInfoFromPullRequest({ repo: options.repo, ... })` is still called without `isValidRepoSlug(options.repo)`. If the goal is to harden against untrusted `repo` strings, validate once before either branch (and fail fast) so the PR and commit paths are consistent.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull request overview This PR migrates webpack’s internal MIME type handling from the external `mime-types` package to direct use of `mime-db`, aiming to reduce dependency size and update an outdated dependency. **Changes:** - Replace `mime-types` dependency with `mime-db` and update TypeScript type packages accordingly. - Introduce `lib/util/mimeTypes.js` as an internal helper implementing `lookup()`/`extension()` on top of `mime-db`. - Update `AssetGenerator` and `TemplatedPathPlugin` to use the new internal helper. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `package.json` | Swaps `mime-types` for `mime-db` and updates `@types/*` packages. | | `yarn.lock` | Reflects the dependency/type changes and adds `mime-db@^1.54.0` resolution. | | `lib/util/mimeTypes.js` | Adds an internal MIME lookup/extension helper backed by `mime-db`. | | `lib/TemplatedPathPlugin.js` | Uses internal MIME helper for data-URI extension inference. | | `lib/asset/AssetGenerator.js` | Uses internal MIME helper for data URL MIME type resolution; updates JSDoc type. | | `.changeset/full-months-jog.md` | Declares a patch changeset for the migration. |

With this change, running tests will throw a module-not-found error when those configs are loaded. Update the test fixtures to use the new internal `lib/util/mimeTypes` helper (or another local helper) instead, or keep `mime-types` as a (dev)dependency if the fixtures are intended to exercise the external package.

body: `runFixSpecialAsync()` is only executed when `hasSymlink` is false. With this PR removing/ignoring the `fix:special` outputs, a repo that already has the webpack symlink (i.e. `hasSymlink === true`) can end up with missing `types.d.ts` and `schemas/**/*.check.*` files after pulling, and `yarn setup`/`yarn lint` won’t regenerate them. Consider running `fix:special` unconditionally (after ensuring deps), or gating it on the presence of the generated artifacts rather than the symlink check.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This change removes `lint:special`, but the repo/runtime still depends on `fix:special` artifacts (e.g. `types.d.ts` is the published `types` entry, and many `lib/*` modules `require("../schemas/**.check")`). Without a replacement step (e.g. a `prepack`/`prepublishOnly` script or CI/release workflow step) to generate these files before packaging/publishing, consumers (and any tooling expecting `types.d.ts`) will get a broken package.

body:
`actions/checkout` is now pulling `webpack/.github` without a pinned `ref`, so the workflow’s behavior (especially `require-pr-template`) can change whenever that repo’s default branch updates. In this repo we already pin cross-repo checkouts to a specific commit (see `.github/workflows/test.yml`), so it’d be better to add an explicit `ref` here as well (and update it intentionally when the template changes).
```suggestion
          repository: webpack/.github
          ref: <PINNED_WEBPACK_DOT_GITHUB_COMMIT_SHA>
```

body:
## Pull request overview This PR removes the direct dependency on `json-parse-even-better-errors` by introducing an internal `parseJson` utility and updating webpack internals and option typings to use it, with new unit tests to validate parsing and error formatting behavior. **Changes:** - Replace `json-parse-even-better-errors` usage in core modules with `lib/util/parseJson`. - Remove the direct dependency and related type/module declarations; update schema/type references to the new internal parse function type. - Add unit tests covering parsing behavior (including BOM handling) and error message/metadata expectations. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 10 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | lib/util/parseJson.js | New internal JSON parsing helper with enhanced error formatting. | | lib/NormalModule.js | Switch loader-options JSON parsing to internal `parseJson`. | | lib/json/JsonParser.js | Use internal `parseJson` as default JSON parser implementation. | | lib/DllReferencePlugin.js | Switch manifest JSON parsing to internal `parseJson` and adjust imports. | | lib/Compiler.js | Switch records JSON parsing to internal `parseJson`. | | test/parseJson.unittest.js | Add unit tests for `parseJson` parsing + error behavior. | | package.json | Remove direct dependency on `json-parse-even-better-errors`. | | yarn.lock | Lockfile update reflecting dependency removal as a direct dependency. | | declarations.d.ts | Remove module declaration for `json-parse-even-better-errors`. | | schemas/WebpackOptions.json | Point `JsonParserOptions.parse` tsType to internal `ParseJsonFn`. | | declarations/WebpackOptions.d.ts | Update `JsonParserOptions.parse` typing to internal `ParseJsonFn`. | | types.d.ts | Update `JsonParserOptions.parse` typing and introduce `ParseJsonFn` interface. | | .github/dependabot.yml | Stop excluding `json-parse-even-better-errors` from dependabot updates. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `parseJson` always wraps the caught value as `Error` via a JSDoc cast, but `JSON.parse` will rethrow whatever a user-provided `reviver` throws (which may be a non-Error). In that case `JSONParseError` will access `err.message` and can throw a TypeError, masking the real failure. Please defensively normalize `err` to an `Error` (or handle non-Error values) before reading `.message` and before assigning `systemError`.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
The schema now points `JsonParserOptions.parse` at `ParseJsonFn`, but that type currently excludes `Buffer` returns. Since webpack’s JSON pipeline accepts `Buffer` results (via `JsonData`), the generated config typings will be narrower than runtime behavior. Consider updating the referenced type (or the type itself) so the schema-generated typings include `Buffer`.
```suggestion
          "tsType": "((...args: Parameters<import('../lib/util/parseJson').ParseJsonFn>) => ReturnType<import('../lib/util/parseJson').ParseJsonFn> | Buffer)"
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer
copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull request overview This PR adds first-class handling of CSS `@charset` at-rules within webpack’s CSS module pipeline, ensuring a single normalized `@charset` is emitted at the correct position and warning on conflicts between modules in the same chunk. **Changes:** - Parse `@charset` in `CssParser`, storing the detected charset in `module.buildInfo` and removing in-module occurrences (non-`style` exportType). - Emit a single `@charset` header at the beginning of rendered CSS chunks and warn when charsets conflict across modules in a chunk. - Add a dedicated `css/charset` config case + snapshot updates; extend typing/docs for the new `buildInfo.charset` field. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 27 out of 28 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/css/CssParser.js` | Adds parsing/removal of `@charset` and stores normalized charset in `buildInfo`. | | `lib/css/CssModulesPlugin.js` | Detects conflicting charsets (warnings) and prepends a single `@charset` to chunk CSS output. | | `lib/css/CssGenerator.js` | Prepends `@charset` for `css-style-sheet` exportType output where applicable. | | `lib/Module.js` | Documents `buildInfo.charset` in JSDoc. | | `types.d.ts` | Adds `charset?: string` to build info typing. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/webpack.config.js` | New test case configuration covering multiple exportTypes with `@charset`. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/test.config.js` | Test harness setup for loading emitted CSS in the browser-like environment. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/warnings.js` | Asserts warning output for conflicting `@charset` values. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/index.js` | New runtime assertions/snapshots for link/text/style/css-style-sheet outputs. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/styles-1.link.css` | Fixture: UTF-8 charset + imports. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/styles-2.link.css` | Fixture: utf-8 charset variant for normalization test. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/styles-3.link.css` | Fixture: conflicting/unknown charset to trigger warning. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/styles-4.text.css` | Fixture: text exportType with charset + imports. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/styles-5.css-style-sheet.css` | Fixture: css-style-sheet exportType with charset + imports. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/styles-6.style.css` | Fixture: style exportType with charset + import. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/import.link.css` | Fixture: imported link CSS with charset. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/import.text.css` | Fixture: imported text CSS with charset + nested imports. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/import-nested.text.css` | Fixture: nested import text CSS with charset. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/__snapshots__/ConfigTest.snap` | Adds snapshots for the new `css/charset` config case. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/__snapshots__/ConfigCacheTest.snap` | Cache-mode snapshots for the new `css/charset` config case. | | `test/configCases/css/mini-css-extract-plugin/__snapshots__/ConfigTest.snap` | Updates expected output to a single hoisted `@charset`. | | `test/configCases/css/mini-css-extract-plugin/__snapshots__/ConfigCacheTest.snap` | Cache-mode snapshot updates for hoisted `@charset`. | | `test/configCases/css/css-modules/style.module.css` | Fixture changes (class rename + additional declarations) impacting snapshots. | | `test/configCases/css/css-modules/__snapshots__/ConfigTest.snap` | Snapshot updates reflecting CSS modules fixture changes. | | `test/configCases/css/css-modules/__snapshots__/ConfigCacheTest.snap` | Cache-mode snapshot updates reflecting fixture changes. | | `test/configCases/css/pure-css/__snapshots__/ConfigTest.snap` | Snapshot updates reflecting fixture changes. | | `test/configCases/css/pure-css/__snapshots__/ConfigCacheTest.snap` | Cache-mode snapshot updates reflecting fixture changes. | | `.changeset/spicy-buckets-fetch.md` | Declares a patch release note for `@charset` handling. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: In the new `@charset` handler, the callback returns `end` (end of the `@charset` token) instead of advancing to the end of the at-rule. This is inconsistent with other at-rule handlers (e.g. `@namespace`, `@value`) and can cause the tokenizer to continue walking tokens inside the just-removed range, which is unnecessary and can lead to incorrect follow-up parsing in edge cases. Consider returning the semicolon position (or after it) once `@charset` has been processed/removed (including the `!value` path).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `getModulesInOrder` emits charset-conflict warnings while iterating the *unordered* `modules` iterable, and chooses the "expected" charset based on that iteration order. Since ordering is computed afterwards (and is what drives final CSS output), this can make warnings nondeterministic and/or inconsistent with the emitted `@charset` value. Consider performing conflict detection after the final module order is determined (e.g. on the ordered list) so both the warning and output are stable.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
The test title reads "should work handle `@charset` at-rule", which is grammatically incorrect and ends up in snapshot keys. Consider renaming to something like "should handle `@charset` at-rule" (or "should work with `@charset` at-rules") and updating snapshots accordingly.
```suggestion
it("should handle `@charset` at-rule", () => {
```

Consider splitting these unrelated test/fixture updates into a separate PR (or documenting the rationale in the PR description) to keep the change focused and easier to review.

## Pull request overview This PR removes the `strip-ansi` devDependency and replaces its usage in tests with a local helper that prefers Node’s built-in `util.stripVTControlCharacters`, falling back to a regex implementation when unavailable. **Changes:** - Added `test/helpers/stripVTControlCharacters.js` to strip VT/ANSI control characters using `util.stripVTControlCharacters` with a regex fallback. - Updated test helpers/tests to use the new local stripping helper instead of `strip-ansi`. - Removed `strip-ansi` from `package.json` and removed its Dependabot exclusion entry. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | test/helpers/stripVTControlCharacters.js | New helper that strips VT control sequences using Node util when available, otherwise regex. | | test/helpers/captureStdio.js | Switches stripping implementation from `strip-ansi` to the new local helper. | | test/Defaults.unittest.js | Switches stripping implementation from `strip-ansi` to the new local helper. | | package.json | Removes `strip-ansi` from devDependencies. | | .github/dependabot.yml | Removes the `strip-ansi` exclusion entry. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
The fallback regex for stripping VT/ANSI sequences appears to have a typo in the OSC prefix: `\u001B\\]` matches ESC + backslash + `]`, but OSC sequences start with ESC + `]`. This means OSC control sequences (e.g. `\x1B]...\x07`) won’t be stripped on Node versions where `util.stripVTControlCharacters` is unavailable. Update the pattern to match ESC + `]` (e.g. `\u001B\]` or `\u001B]`).
```suggestion
	/(?:\u001B][\s\S]*?(?:\u0007|\u001B\u005C|\u009C))|[\u001B\u009B][[\]()#;?]*(?:\d{1,4}(?:[;:]\d{0,4})*)?[\dA-PR-TZcf-nq-uy=><~]/g;
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: ## Pull request overview Fixes contenthash stability for CSS modules by ensuring additional CSS generator-related options are included in hashing, preventing stale builds when those options change. **Changes:** - Extend `CssGenerator.updateHash` to include `exportType` and `exportsConvention` in the hash input. - Add new `css-generator-options` config cases intended to cover `exportType: "style"` and `"css-style-sheet"`. - Update the test expectation for the number of generated bundles. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 3 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/css/CssGenerator.js` | Adds more option-derived inputs to `updateHash` to avoid silent cache invalidation. | | `test/configCases/contenthash/css-generator-options/webpack.config.js` | Adds new config variants intended to exercise hash changes for different `exportType` values. | | `test/configCases/contenthash/css-generator-options/test.config.js` | Updates bundle count assertion for the added config variants. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `findBundle` unconditionally looks up a CSS asset in `css${i}` and adds it to `allCss`. With the two new configs, if `exportType` results in no emitted `.css` file, `css` becomes `undefined` and `allCss.add(undefined)` will increase the set size, making `expect(allCss.size).toBe(7)` fail. Consider skipping the `allCss.add(...)` when no CSS file is found (or update the expected count to reflect the actual number of emitted CSS assets).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The new test cases set `generator.exportType`, but `CssModulesPlugin` derives `module.exportType` from the *parser* options (`createData.parser.options.exportType`), and `CssGenerator` reads `module.exportType` (it does not read `this.options.exportType`). As written, these configs likely don’t change `module.exportType` and won’t actually exercise the new `updateHash` logic. Set `parser: { exportType: "style" | "css-style-sheet" }` (or wherever the codebase expects this option) so the module’s `exportType` actually changes.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `updateHash` now hashes `this.options.exportsConvention.toString()`. For function-valued `exportsConvention`, this hashes the function source, which can be brittle (e.g. formatting changes) compared to hashing a normalized representation (like the resolved convention name(s)) as done in `CssIcssExportDependency`’s hash. If possible, prefer hashing a stable identifier for the convention (string value, or an explicit `ident` on the function) rather than the full function source.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull request overview Adds new `test/configCases` integration cases to cover previously unexecuted branches in Webpack’s `IgnorePlugin` and `CleanPlugin`, primarily around ignored entry handling, context-based ignoring, and `output.clean.keep` string matching. **Changes:** - Add a config case for an ignored *entry* to exercise `IgnorePlugin`’s `EntryDependency` placeholder module path. - Add a config case for `IgnorePlugin`’s `contextRegExp` behavior during resolution. - Add a config case for `CleanPlugin`’s `output.clean.keep` string branch to preserve matching paths during cleanup. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated 6 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | test/configCases/ignore/ignored-entry/webpack.config.js | Adds a multi-entry setup with `IgnorePlugin` configured to ignore one entry. | | test/configCases/ignore/ignored-entry/index.js | Adds a runtime test scaffold for the ignored-entry case (currently lacks assertions). | | test/configCases/ignore/ignored-entry/ignored-entry.js | Provides the ignored entry module content to validate it is not bundled. | | test/configCases/ignore/ignored-context/webpack.config.js | Configures `IgnorePlugin` with `resourceRegExp` + `contextRegExp`. | | test/configCases/ignore/ignored-context/index.js | Asserts runtime failure when an ignored module is required from a matching context. | | test/configCases/ignore/ignored-context/ignored-folder/module.js | Adds an extra fixture module (currently unused). | | test/configCases/ignore/ignored-context/folder/ignored-sub-module.js | Provides the module intended to be ignored under the matching context. | | test/configCases/ignore/ignored-context/folder/another-module.js | Requires the ignored sub-module to trigger the ignore behavior. | | test/configCases/clean/ignore-string/webpack.config.js | Adds a cleanup test for `output.clean.keep` string prefix matching. | | test/configCases/clean/ignore-string/index.js | Adds a minimal runtime test to satisfy the config-case harness. |

body:
This assertion is tied to a specific runtime error message shape. These messages can vary depending on target/runtime and webpack’s missing-module helpers, making the test brittle. Prefer asserting the throw (`expect(() => require(...)).toThrow()`) or matching only a stable substring (e.g. `/Cannot find module/`) unless you specifically need the exact request string.
```suggestion
	expect(error.message).toMatch(/Cannot find module/);
```

Either remove it to keep the case minimal, or use it as part of a "non-matching context" assertion (with a more specific `contextRegExp`) to validate `contextRegExp` behavior.

body:
`contextRegExp: /folder/` is broad enough to also match directories like `ignored-folder`, which makes it hard to add a meaningful non-matching-context assertion. Consider tightening this (e.g. match the path segment `/folder/` specifically) so the test can distinguish matching vs non-matching contexts.
```suggestion
			contextRegExp: /(^|[\\/])folder([\\/]|$)/
```

body: ## Pull request overview This PR updates the CI integration matrix to run Node.js 10.x macOS jobs on an Intel runner (`macos-13`) instead of `macos-latest` (Apple Silicon), avoiding Rosetta 2 emulation overhead. **Changes:** - Exclude the `macos-latest` + `10.x` integration matrix combination. - Add `macos-13` + `10.x` integration matrix entries for both shards (`part: a` and `part: b`). - Remove the now-unused `calculate_architecture` step and the `actions/setup-node` `architecture` override. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: ## Pull request overview Reduces nondeterminism in the benchmark harness to stabilize CodSpeed simulation/memory runs and local wall-clock results by enforcing required V8 flags, standardizing warmup behavior, improving GC pre-measurement cleanup, and making benchmark registration order deterministic. **Changes:** - Turn missing required V8 flags from a warning into a hard error with guidance to use `yarn benchmark`. - Apply the custom warmup loop in CodSpeed memory mode (async + sync paths). - Serialize benchmark registration (suite-level and shard-level) and add extra GC steps + an event-loop yield before the measured call. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: In the async CodSpeed path, `mongoMeasurement.start(uri)` is called before the two `global.gc()` calls and the event-loop yield. If `mongoMeasurement` is intended to measure only the benchmarked function’s allocations/heap delta, the GCs (and their side-effects) will be included in the measured window. Consider moving `mongoMeasurement.start(uri)` to *after* the GC+drain, immediately before invoking the wrapped benchmark function, so the measurement baseline is taken after cleanup.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The comment says “microtask drain”, but the code awaits `setImmediate`, which schedules a new event-loop turn (macrotask), not a microtask. Either update the comment to match what’s happening, or switch to an actual microtask drain (and, if needed for finalizers, document why an event-loop turn is required).

body:
The text correctly notes EasyCLA matches the commit author *email*, but the next sentence gives examples like `Claude` / `claude-bot` which look like *names* rather than emails. This is internally inconsistent and may mislead readers into thinking the author name affects the check; consider rephrasing the examples to be strictly email identities (or clarify that the issue is the author email / email domain).
```suggestion
EasyCLA matches the **commit author email** to a GitHub account with a signed CLA. A commit using an unrecognized author email such as `claude-bot@users.noreply.github.com`, `noreply@anthropic.com`, or any other email not associated with the requester's GitHub account and signed CLA will fail the CLA check and block the PR.
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
`mcp__github__get_me` is referenced as a way to look up the requester's `USER_ID`, but this tool name isn’t mentioned anywhere else in the guide and is likely environment-specific (and `get_me` typically returns the authenticated user, not an arbitrary requester). To keep the guidance durable, consider either linking to where this MCP tool is defined/available, or replacing it with a generic instruction (e.g., use the GitHub API `/users/<login>` to read the numeric `id`).
```suggestion
2. The requester's GitHub login + their public no-reply email: `<USER_ID>+<login>@users.noreply.github.com` (look up `USER_ID` by reading the numeric `id` from the GitHub REST API `/users/<login>` response).
```

body: ## Pull request overview This PR fixes nondeterministic Module Federation output that can cause runtime and entry bundle content-hashes to drift between otherwise-identical builds (especially when absolute project roots differ across CI runners). **Changes:** - Make `ProvideSharedModule.identifier()` path-normalizable by `makePathsRelative` via `|`-separated segments. - Emit `ConsumeSharedRuntimeModule`’s consume-shared module lists in stable, id-sorted order using `getOrderedChunkModulesIterableBySourceType(..., compareModulesById(...))`. - Add targeted tests: a unit test for identifier normalization and a config-case asserting stable runtime mapping order. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/sharing/ProvideSharedModule.js` | Switch identifier delimiter to `|` so embedded absolute requests can be normalized for deterministic IDs. | | `lib/sharing/ConsumeSharedRuntimeModule.js` | Use ordered chunk-module iteration with an id comparator to stabilize runtime emission order. | | `test/SharingUtil.unittest.js` | Add unit coverage ensuring `makePathsRelative` produces stable identifiers across differing build roots. | | `test/configCases/sharing/consume-shared-stable-runtime/*` | Add a config case validating that `moduleToHandlerMapping` is emitted in sorted id order (and runtime/main bundles execute). | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. body:
## Pull request overview Refactors webpack’s DLL-related implementation into a dedicated `lib/dll/` area and exposes DLL plugins under a new `webpack.dll` namespace, aligning consumption with the public `compiler.webpack.*` surface. **Changes:** - Added `exports.dll` namespace to TypeScript types and a corresponding `dll` scope on the main JS export object. - Updated DLL plugin/module implementations to use correct relative imports after moving into `lib/dll/`. - Updated internal serializer registration entries for moved DLL modules. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 12 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `types.d.ts` | Adds `exports.dll` namespace and adjusts export placement for DLL plugins. | | `lib/util/internalSerializables.js` | Moves serializer loader entries for DLL modules to `dll/*` keys. | | `lib/index.js` | Adds `dll` scope and routes DLL plugin getters to `./dll/*`. | | `lib/dll/LibManifestPlugin.js` | Fixes relative imports after moving under `lib/dll/`. | | `lib/dll/DllReferencePlugin.js` | Fixes relative imports and schema require paths after move. | | `lib/dll/DllPlugin.js` | Fixes relative imports and schema require paths after move. | | `lib/dll/DllModuleFactory.js` | Fixes relative imports/types after move. | | `lib/dll/DllModule.js` | Updates imports and changes serialization request id to `webpack/lib/dll/DllModule`. | | `lib/dll/DllEntryPlugin.js` | Fixes dependency imports/types after move. | | `lib/dll/DelegatedPlugin.js` | Fixes dependency import/types after move. | | `lib/dll/DelegatedModuleFactoryPlugin.js` | Fixes typedef import paths after move. | | `lib/dll/DelegatedModule.js` | Updates imports and changes serialization request id to `webpack/lib/dll/DelegatedModule`. |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (2) **lib/dll/DllModule.js:184** * Changing the `makeSerializable` request from `webpack/lib/DllModule` to `webpack/lib/dll/DllModule` changes the serialization identifier for this class. That will make existing persistent caches (e.g. filesystem cache packs) from earlier builds fail to deserialize with `No deserializer registered...` errors, even if they would otherwise be invalidated by build dependency snapshots. Consider keeping the original request string for backward compatibility (and just update `internalSerializables` to require the new file location), or provide a compatibility layer that still registers/deserializes the old request id. **lib/dll/DelegatedModule.js:290** * Changing the `makeSerializable` request from `webpack/lib/DelegatedModule` to `webpack/lib/dll/DelegatedModule` changes the serialization identifier for this class and can break deserialization of existing persistent caches (filesystem cache packs) created with older builds. To avoid cache restore failures, consider keeping the original request string and updating `internalSerializables` to point to the new module location (or otherwise maintaining backward-compatible deserialization for the old request id).
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Removing the `DelegatedModule` and `DllModule` entries here can break restoring older persistent caches that were serialized with request ids like `webpack/lib/DelegatedModule` / `webpack/lib/DllModule`. If you intend to preserve cache backward compatibility, keep these keys (and have them require the new `../dll/*` locations) even after moving the implementation files.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Adding only the new `dll/*` internal serializable keys is not enough to keep older caches working if the serialization request ids changed (because `ObjectMiddleware.register` only allows one request id per constructor). Either keep the original `makeSerializable` request ids (preferred for cache compatibility), or ensure the old request ids are still supported/aliased during deserialization.
```suggestion

	DelegatedModule: () => require("../dll/DelegatedModule"),
	DllModule: () => require("../dll/DllModule"),
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull request overview This PR removes the published-type dependency on `@types/eslint-scope` by inlining the needed `eslint-scope` typings into webpack’s own type surface, and updates internal JSDoc typedef imports accordingly. **Changes:** - Move `@types/eslint-scope` from `dependencies` to `devDependencies` and stop referencing it from the published `types.d.ts`. - Add local `Scope` / `Reference` / `Variable` type definitions and re-point internal JSDoc typedef imports to webpack-owned types. - Update several tooling/dependency versions (`assemblyscript`, `loader-runner`, `terser-webpack-plugin`, `terser`) and regenerate `yarn.lock`. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | yarn.lock | Regenerated lockfile reflecting dependency/version updates. | | types.d.ts | Removes `eslint-scope` type import; adds webpack-owned scope/reference/variable typings and renames the webpack `node` option type alias. | | package.json | Moves `@types/eslint-scope` to devDeps; bumps several dependency versions. | | lib/util/concatenate.js | Switches JSDoc typedef imports from `eslint-scope` to `JavascriptModulesPlugin`-provided types. | | lib/optimize/ConcatenatedModule.js | Switches JSDoc typedef imports from `eslint-scope` to `JavascriptModulesPlugin`-provided types. | | lib/javascript/JavascriptModulesPlugin.js | Introduces local JSDoc typedefs for `Scope` / `Reference` / `Variable` (temporary replacement for `@types/eslint-scope`). | | .changeset/sour-hornets-admire.md | Adds a patch changeset documenting removal of `@types/eslint-scope` from runtime dependencies. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
These JSDoc typedef imports point at `../javascript/JavascriptModulesPlugin`, but that module also requires `../util/concatenate` at runtime. Even though this is type-only, it creates a circular type dependency and couples a low-level util to a high-level plugin. Consider moving `Scope`/`Reference`/`Variable` typedefs into a small dedicated module (that doesn’t import `concatenate`) and importing from there instead.
```suggestion
/**
 * @typedef {object} Reference
 * @property {*} [identifier]
 * @property {*} [from]
 * @property {*} [resolved]
 */
/**
 * @typedef {object} Scope
 * @property {Scope[]} childScopes
 * @property {Variable[]} variables
 */
/**
 * @typedef {object} Variable
 * @property {Reference[]} references
 * @property {Node[]} identifiers
 * @property {Scope} scope
 */
```

body: ## Pull request overview This PR updates the repository’s PR quality GitHub Actions workflow to delegate checks to the organization’s shared reusable workflow, rather than maintaining the full anti-slop job inline. **Changes:** - Switch `anti-slop` job implementation to `uses: webpack/.github/.github/workflows/pr-quality.yml@...`. - Keep the workflow triggered via `pull_request_target` (currently only for `opened` and `reopened` events). --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
`pull_request_target` is currently limited to `opened` and `reopened`, so these quality checks won’t re-run when a PR is updated (e.g., new commits pushed via `synchronize` or title edited via `edited`). For conventional-commit/title enforcement this typically needs at least `synchronize` and `edited` as well.
```suggestion
    types: [opened, reopened, synchronize, edited]
```
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This job now delegates to a reusable workflow. If that shared workflow posts PR comments / closes PRs (as the previous inline `anti-slop` step did), it will need the appropriate token permissions; this workflow currently grants `issues: read` only. Consider bumping to `issues: write` (or whichever specific permissions the shared workflow documents) to avoid permission errors at runtime.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | types.d.ts | Updates generated TS declarations for module/cache error & warning typing. | | lib/errors/NonErrorEmittedError.js | New extracted error class with serialization registration. | | lib/errors/HookWebpackError.js | Updates callback typing in JSdoc to `Error \| null`. | | lib/WebpackOptionsApply.js | Changes `RequireJsStuffPlugin` require path used when AMD is enabled. | | lib/Watching.js | Updates error typing in JSdoc and removes unused typedef. | | lib/NormalModule.js | Switches to the extracted `NonErrorEmittedError` and updates error typing annotations. | | lib/Module.js | Broadens warnings/errors storage and APIs to `Error[]` in JSdoc. | | lib/Compilation.js | Adjusts typing around `module.getErrors()/getWarnings()` usage via casts. | | lib/Cache.js | Updates cache callback JSdoc error typing and description. | | .changeset/violet-news-add.md | Declares a patch release for the typing fix. |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **lib/Compilation.js:3222** * `module.getErrors()` / `getWarnings()` are now typed to return `Error[]`, but these are cast to `WebpackError[]` here. If any callers add plain `Error` objects to a module, this cast is incorrect/misleading; consider using a narrower intersection type for the `.module` assignment (e.g. `Error & { module?: Module }`) or updating the upstream types so `getErrors()` is `WebpackError[]` if that’s the real invariant. ``` const errors = /** @type {WebpackError[]} */ (module.getErrors()); if (errors !== undefined) { for (const error of errors) { if (!error.module) { error.module = module; } ```

body: This test relies on Node’s `SourceMap` consumer (`__NodeSourceMap`) and calls `findEntry(0, 0)`. That ties the config case to newer Node versions and can be brittle if there is no mapping at generated (0,0) (or if the API’s line/column base differs), causing failures unrelated to CSS source map propagation. Consider removing this consumer check and relying on the existing structural assertions + `decodeAllMappings` + `sourcesContent` marker checks (which are Node-version agnostic).

body: `parseString` now uses `JSON.stringify(str.slice(1, -1))` for single-quoted and template-literal specifiers. This preserves backslash escapes instead of interpreting them, so e.g. a specifier like `\"` or `\'` will remain in the returned string rather than becoming `"` / `'` as JavaScript string-literal semantics dictate. That can lead to resolving/tracking the wrong build dependency path (or failing to resolve) compared to what the module specifier actually evaluates to. Consider converting single-quoted/template literals into a valid JSON string while *preserving* escape semantics (e.g. translate `\'`/`\`` appropriately and then `JSON.parse`), or use a small string-literal parser so the returned value matches JS evaluation.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: @copilot apply changes based on [this feedback](https://github.com/webpack/webpack/pull/20890#discussion_r3160235172)
author: alexander-akait

body:
Fixed in b4a4683. The `parseString` function now uses a single-pass regex `\\([\s\S])|"` to atomically process escape sequences, avoiding the double-escaping issue from `JSON.stringify`:
- Single-quoted strings: `\'` → `'`, other `\X` sequences pass through unchanged, unescaped `"` → `\"`
- Template literals: `\`` → `` ` `` and `\'` → `'`, other `\X` sequences pass through unchanged, unescaped `"` → `\"`

This preserves correct JS escape semantics (e.g. `\n` remains `\n` for `JSON.parse` to interpret as a newline, `\\` stays as `\\` for a literal backslash).

body: Removing all newlines from template literals (`q === "`") changes JavaScript semantics (newlines are literal characters in template strings). This can cause build dependency tracking to resolve/track a different path than the module specifier actually evaluates to. Consider preserving newlines (or only handling line continuations like `\\\n`) and letting invalid specifiers fail/skip rather than rewriting them.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The escape handling in `parseString` only covers a small subset of JS escapes (e.g. `\n`, `\t`) and will mis-decode valid escapes like `\xNN`, `\uNNNN` and `\u{...}` (e.g. `"\x41"` becomes `"x41"`). This can lead to incorrect dependency paths being tracked. Consider implementing proper JS string escape parsing for these forms (and `\\\r?\n` line continuations in string literals) or delegating to a parser that matches JS semantics.

body:
Fixed in f572e8d. `parseString` now uses a proper character-by-character parser loop that correctly handles all JS string escape sequences:
- `\xNN` hex escapes (e.g. `"\x41"` → `"A"`)
- `\uNNNN` unicode escapes (e.g. `"\u0041"` → `"A"`)
- `\u{N...}` unicode code point escapes (e.g. `"\u{1F600}"` → `"😀"`)
- `\\\n` / `\\\r\n` line continuations
- Simple named escapes (`\n`, `\r`, `\t`, etc.)

All multi-character forms include validation — malformed sequences (invalid hex digits, out-of-range code points `> 0x10FFFF`, missing closing `}`) fall back to using the literal character rather than producing `NaN` or throwing. Template literal literal newlines are also preserved now instead of being stripped.

body:
## Pull request overview This PR updates webpack’s hashing paths to take advantage of `webpack-sources@^3.4.1`’s new `Source.prototype.buffers()` API, aiming to reduce peak memory usage and improve performance when hashing large concatenated/modified sources. **Changes:** - Bump `webpack-sources` from `^3.3.4` to `^3.4.1`. - Add `updateHashFromSource(hash, source)` helper to hash `Source` content via `buffers()` when available (fallback to `buffer()`). - Rework `RealContentHashPlugin` dedup/hash input flow to operate on buffer chunk arrays without concatenating. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | yarn.lock | Updates lockfile entry for `webpack-sources@^3.4.1`. | | package.json | Bumps `webpack-sources` dependency to `^3.4.1`. | | lib/util/source.js | Introduces `updateHashFromSource` helper and exports it. | | lib/asset/AssetGenerator.js | Uses `updateHashFromSource` for full content hashing. | | lib/dependencies/CssIcssExportDependency.js | Uses `updateHashFromSource` for CSS `[contenthash]`-based ident hashing. | | lib/optimize/RealContentHashPlugin.js | Avoids forced concatenation by deduping/processing `Source` buffer chunks. | | .changeset/webpack-sources-buffers.md | Adds a changeset entry documenting the perf/memory improvement. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `assetsContent` passed into `hooks.updateHash` used to contain one `Buffer` per unique asset source (deduped). This change flattens chunk arrays returned by `source.buffers()` so a single asset may contribute multiple entries, which changes the observable hook contract (e.g. existing `updateHash` taps that rely on `input.length === 1` for a single unique source will stop working). To keep backwards compatibility, consider preserving the per-source `Buffer[]` shape when calling the hook (or only using chunked hashing when there are no taps), or explicitly updating the hook API/typedef + in-repo usages/tests to handle chunked inputs as a concatenated byte stream.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Good catch. Fixed in `ec06ae0`: the `Buffer[]` shape passed to `hooks.updateHash` is preserved (one entry per unique asset, exactly as before), but the array is only built when the hook is actually used (`hooks.updateHash.isUsed()`). When nobody is tapped — the common case — we feed chunks directly into the internal hash and skip the per-asset `Buffer.concat` entirely. Contracts seen by existing taps stay identical; the memory win is preserved on the default path.

---

## Pull request overview This PR fixes `library: { type: "module" }` outputs that were unnecessarily emitting `__webpack_require__` and related runtime helpers in the “single concatenated entry inlined without an IIFE” shape, even though the on-demand exports source that introduced those requirements is not re-emitted. **Changes:** - Track stashed on-demand exports source per runtime on concatenated modules and use it to decide when runtime globals can be dropped. - Add a `ModuleLibraryPlugin` hook to remove now-unused chunk runtime requirements for the inline-single-entry case. - Update config case assertions and snapshots to ensure the extra runtime prelude is not emitted; add a changeset. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/library/ModuleLibraryPlugin.js` | Drops unused runtime globals for inline single-entry module-library output based on stashed exports-source metadata. | | `test/configCases/library/module-useless-export-requirement/entry2.js` | Tightens bundle-content assertions by only scanning the prelude and checking for absence of runtime helpers. | | `test/configCases/library/module-useless-export-requirement/entry3.js` | Same as `entry2.js`, applied to the other entry variant. | | `test/configCases/library/concatenate-modules-named-import-externals/__snapshots__/ConfigTest.snap` | Updates snapshot to reflect removal of the unnecessary runtime prelude. | | `test/configCases/library/concatenate-modules-named-import-externals/__snapshots__/ConfigCacheTest.snap` | Same snapshot update for cached compilation runs. | | `.changeset/drop-unused-on-demand-runtime-helpers.md` | Adds a patch-level changelog entry describing the runtime helper removal. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: In `additionalChunkRuntimeRequirements`, the code deletes `RuntimeGlobals.exports` and `RuntimeGlobals.requireScope` whenever the entry module source doesn’t contain a `__webpack_require__.d(` call. However, a concatenated module can still reference `__webpack_exports__` without calling `definePropertyGetters` (e.g. when `ConcatenatedModule` emits the ESM compat flag via `__webpack_require__.r(__webpack_exports__)`, or other non-`.d` uses of `__webpack_exports__`). In those cases removing `RuntimeGlobals.exports` prevents `var __webpack_exports__ = {};` from being emitted and will break the generated bundle at runtime. Please also gate deletion on the generated JS source not referencing `RuntimeGlobals.exports` / `RuntimeGlobals.makeNamespaceObject` (or only delete `definePropertyGetters` and leave `exports` unless you can prove it’s unused).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Good catch. The bail-out now matches `__webpack_require__.` (with the dot) instead of just `__webpack_require__.d(`, so any helper call in the codegen source — `.r(__webpack_exports__)` from the ESM compat flag, namespace objects, deferred externals, etc. — keeps `exports` and `requireScope` in the chunk's set. The `__webpack_exports__` reference Copilot mentions is itself written as `__webpack_require__.r(__webpack_exports__)` in `runtimeTemplate.defineEsModuleFlagStatement`, so the new check covers it. Fixed in 4548a8d.

---

## Pull request overview This PR lays groundwork for future native HTML entry support in webpack 6 by introducing an `experiments.html` flag and reserving `html` as a core module/source type, following the existing experimental-feature pattern used elsewhere in the codebase. **Changes:** - Added `experiments.html` to the webpack config schema, generated typings, CLI option snapshots, and release notes. - Introduced `HTML_MODULE_TYPE` / `HTML_TYPE` constants and a new `HtmlModulesPlugin`. - Wired the new plugin behind `options.experiments.html` in `WebpackOptionsApply`. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 10 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | types.d.ts | Adds generated public typings for `experiments.html`. | | test/statsCases/logging/__snapshots__/StatsTest.snap | Updates logging stats snapshot output. | | test/statsCases/logging-debug/__snapshots__/StatsTest.snap | Updates debug logging stats snapshot output. | | test/__snapshots__/Cli.basictest.js.snap | Adds CLI snapshot coverage for the new experiment flag. | | schemas/WebpackOptions.json | Adds schema entries for `experiments.html`. | | lib/html/HtmlModulesPlugin.js | Adds placeholder plugin that taps parser/generator hooks for `html`. | | lib/config/defaults.js | Adds the new experiment default. | | lib/WebpackOptionsApply.js | Applies `HtmlModulesPlugin` when the experiment is enabled. | | lib/ModuleTypeConstants.js | Registers the new `html` module type constant. | | lib/ModuleSourceTypeConstants.js | Registers the new `html` source type constant/set. | | declarations/WebpackOptions.d.ts | Adds generated declaration typings for `experiments.html`. | | .changeset/experiments-html-foundation.md | Adds release note describing the new experimental flag. |

body: This tap does not return a parser or generator instance. `NormalModuleFactory.createParser/createGenerator` treats a falsy result as an error and throws `No parser registered for html` / `No generator registered for html`, so enabling `experiments.html` still breaks any rule that resolves to `type: "html"`.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The new experiment flag is only defaulted here; there is still no default rule that maps `.html` resources (or `text/html`) to the new `html` module type. With the current defaults, `entry: './index.html'` will not become a first-class HTML module, so the advertised entry-point support never activates.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This description says `.html` files can be used directly as entry points, but this PR does not actually wire any default rule or working parser/generator for HTML yet. As written, the user-facing CLI/schema docs promise functionality that still fails when the flag is enabled.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: There is no Defaults.unittest update covering the new `experiments.html` default, even though that suite already snapshots the normalized `experiments` object and `module.defaultRules`. Without a defaults test here, regressions like a missing `.html` rule or an unexpected default value can slip in unnoticed.

## Pull request overview This PR fixes missing JS source map entries for CSS Modules using the default `exportType: "link"` by ensuring the generated JS wrapper for class exports is emitted as an `OriginalSource` (matching other CSS export types), and adds a regression test + changeset entry. **Changes:** - Wrap link-type CSS module JS exports in `OriginalSource` so the wrapper appears in the bundle JS source map (with `sources`/`sourcesContent`/mappings). - Add a new config-case regression test covering JS source map presence for `style.module.css` under link export type. - Add a patch changeset describing the fix. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 5 out of 5 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/css/CssGenerator.js` | Wraps link-type CSS module JS export output in `OriginalSource` so it is represented in the JS source map. | | `test/configCases/source-map/css-modules-link-source-map/webpack.config.js` | Adds a dedicated config-case enabling `experiments.css` + `devtool: "source-map"` for the regression test. | | `test/configCases/source-map/css-modules-link-source-map/index.js` | Adds assertions that the JS source map includes an entry for the CSS module source. | | `test/configCases/source-map/css-modules-link-source-map/style.module.css` | Provides CSS module input used by the new regression test. | | `.changeset/css-link-source-map.md` | Adds a patch-level changelog entry for the source map fix. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The test comment says the generated CSS-module JS should appear as `sourcesContent`, but the assertions only check `sources` and that `mappings` is non-empty (which can be true due to other modules like `index.js`). Consider asserting that `sourcesContent` exists and that the entry corresponding to the matched `cssSource` contains the generated export wrapper (e.g. `module.exports` / `exports = { ... }`) so the test actually verifies the behavior this PR is fixing.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Good catch — strengthened in 7505c06: the test now also asserts `sourcesContent` is an array with a non-empty string at the index that matches the CSS module's `sources` entry, so it can no longer pass purely on contributions from other modules like `index.js`.

The exact contents of that `sourcesContent` entry shifted in the follow-up commit fc6d48d: when a CSS module emits class exports we now build a `SourceMapSource` whose single source is the original `.css` file with its real CSS content as `sourcesContent`, instead of an `OriginalSource` carrying the generated JS wrapper. That gives each `"btn":` line in the bundle a fine-grained mapping back to the corresponding `.btn { ... }` selector — same fidelity as `css-loader`. There's a separate test (`css-modules-link-fine-grained-mappings`) that verifies the exact line/column resolution end-to-end via `SourceMapConsumer`. So this PR's test stays intentionally loose about the *content* (CSS vs. JS wrapper) and just checks that something meaningful is there.

body: `test.filter.js` currently restricts this config case to Node >= 22 based on a comment about using native `import("./mod.wasm")` as a reference via `webpackIgnore: true`, but the actual test uses `WebAssembly.instantiate(referenceBytes)` and does not perform any native wasm `import()`. This makes the filter/comment inconsistent and unnecessarily skips the test on older Node versions that should still be able to run it (as long as `supportsWebAssembly()` is true). Consider either updating the filter/comment to match the current reference approach, or reintroducing the native-import reference if Node >= 22 is truly required.

author: alexander-akait

body: @xiaoxiaojx Let's set `needIdInConcatenation` when it is a css module, I think checking `needIdInConcatenation` should be enough and less checks
author: alexander-akait

body:
There’s a subtle difference between `isCssModule` and `needIdInConcatenation` here.

`isCssModule` means the module always needs an id, regardless of the scenario.
`needIdInConcatenation` only means the id is required during concatenation.

For example, during the HMR phase, with `isCssModule` we don’t need to traverse child modules, but for `needIdInConcatenation` we still do.

body: ## Pull request overview This PR updates webpack’s CSS generator so `exportType: "text"` preserves `@charset`, matching the existing behavior for `css-style-sheet` exports, and adjusts composed text imports to avoid duplicate leading charset directives. **Changes:** - Prepend `@charset` for CSS modules exported as text. - Strip a leading `@charset` from directly imported text exports when composing a text export. - Update charset snapshots and add a patch changeset entry. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/css/CssGenerator.js` | Adds charset preservation for text exports and trims imported text prefixes during concatenation. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/__snapshots__/ConfigTest.snap` | Updates expected text-export snapshots to include the preserved charset. | | `test/configCases/css/charset/__snapshots__/ConfigCacheTest.snap` | Mirrors the snapshot updates for cached config test cases. | | `.changeset/css-modules-text-export-charset.md` | Documents the patch-level behavior change for text exports. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This only strips a dependency's prefix when the dependency itself declares `@charset`. A transitive case still breaks: if `a.text.css` has no local `@charset` but imports `b.text.css` (which does), `a`'s default export will still start with `@charset`, yet `a.buildInfo.charset` is `undefined`, so importing `a` from a text module with its own charset will leave a second `@charset` in the middle of the final string.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Good catch — fixed in 03ea920. The slice/prepend now use an *effective* charset that walks through text imports when a module has no local `@charset`, and the test fixtures (`leaf-with-charset.text.css` → `inherit-charset.text.css` → `styles-7.text.css`) cover both the inherited case directly and inherited-then-imported-by-charset-owner case.

body:
## Pull request overview This PR improves webpack’s `import defer * as ns` behavior to better align with the TC39 import-defer proposal, particularly around (1) not triggering evaluation on `ns.x = value`, and (2) deferred-namespace identity being stable and distinct from eager namespaces. **Changes:** - Adjust parsing of simple `ns.foo = value` assignments so webpack doesn’t rewrite them via the eager-evaluating `.a` getter path. - Add a new runtime-level deferred-namespace cache to share a single Deferred Module Namespace proxy per module across defer-import call sites. - Update tests/known-skips to reflect the improved spec compliance (with production-concatenation still listed as problematic for identity). ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `test/test262.spectest.js` | Unskips defer `[[Set]]` / identity tests where fixed; keeps identity skipped for production concatenation. | | `test/configCases/defer-import/same-module/index.js` | Updates expectation: eager and deferred namespaces for same module must not be the same object. | | `lib/runtime/MakeDeferredNamespaceObjectRuntime.js` | Caches deferred namespace proxy per module and avoids returning underlying exports for `mode & 8`. | | `lib/javascript/JavascriptModulesPlugin.js` | Emits new `__webpack_module_deferred_namespace_cache__` runtime global when needed. | | `lib/dependencies/HarmonyImportDependencyParserPlugin.js` | Special-cases `ns.foo = value` for defer imports to avoid eager evaluation via `.a`. | | `.changeset/defer-import-spec-compliant-set-and-identity.md` | Adds a patch changeset describing the behavior change. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The deferred-namespace cache is keyed only by `moduleId`, but this function still accepts a `mode` argument and there are call sites that can pass different mode values for the same module (e.g. `| 16` in `RuntimeTemplate.moduleNamespacePromise`, or differing exportsType/strictness decisions). Returning the first cached object unconditionally can yield incorrect namespace shape/semantics for later calls with a different `mode`. Consider keying the cache by `(moduleId, mode)` (or storing `{ mode, ns }` and validating `mode` matches before reusing).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: ## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 13 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The cache key masks off bit 16 (`shapeMode = mode & ~16`), but bit 16 changes `createFakeNamespaceObject` behavior for promise-like/thenable exports. With the current cache key, whichever call site runs first (mode with/without 16) will determine the cached deferred namespace behavior for all call sites, which can make static `import defer` and dynamic `import.defer` observe different wrapping semantics than requested. Consider either including bit 16 in the cache key, or normalizing `mode` (masking off 16) before any `createFakeNamespaceObject` usage so caching across call sites can’t change semantics.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This caches the result under `shapeMode` but passes the original `mode` through to `createFakeNamespaceObject`. If callers differ only by bit 16, they’ll share the same cached object while potentially initializing it with different `createFakeNamespaceObject` semantics (thenable short-circuit). Normalizing `mode` to `shapeMode` before this call would make the caching behavior consistent.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: ## Pull request overview Copilot reviewed 13 out of 13 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Because the deferred namespace Proxy is now cached globally (`byMode[mode] = new Proxy(...)`), any mutation of the handler during `init()` is shared across *all* defer-import call sites. In particular, `init()` deletes `handler.set` for `mode & 8`, which means after the first property read triggers `init()`, later `ns.x = value` can start succeeding (creating properties on `ns_target`) instead of consistently failing/throwing as a Module Namespace `[[Set]]` should. Consider keeping the `set` trap even after init (or making the target non-extensible) so `[[Set]]` remains reliably non-writable regardless of whether evaluation has already happened.

body: In `createSnapshot` (context timestamps, mode 1), `cacheUsable` only filters out existence-only (`{}`) entries. If watchpack provides an `OnlySafeTimeEntry` like `{ safeTime }` for a directory, `getResolvedTimestamp` will return it and it will be written into `contextTimestamps` without a `timestampHash`. Since snapshot validity for contexts relies on `timestampHash` comparisons (safeTime is not compared to the snapshot), this can produce snapshots that miss directory-change detection. Consider treating cached context entries that are non-null but lack `timestampHash` as a cache miss here too (similar to the `cacheLacksHash` logic used in snapshot validation) and force `_readFreshContextTimestamp` so the snapshot stores a complete entry.

body: ## Pull request overview This PR updates webpack’s Test262 harness skip list to remove duplicates and document known-spec divergences more clearly, and it also includes two small parser fixes (with changesets) to improve dynamic `import()` handling and reject invalid `new import.*(...)` forms. **Changes:** - Clean up `test/test262.spectest.js` known-bugs entries (remove duplicates) and add root-cause comments for skipped groups. - `JavascriptParser`: evaluate side-effect-free `SequenceExpression` tails to allow resolving `import((1, 0, "./mod.js"))`. - `JavascriptParser`: reject `new import.defer(...)` / `new import.source(...)` as a parse-time `SyntaxError`, and add changesets for both user-facing fixes. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `test/test262.spectest.js` | Removes duplicate skip entries and expands comments explaining why certain Test262 cases remain skipped. | | `lib/javascript/JavascriptParser.js` | Adds a `SequenceExpression` evaluator for safe folding, and throws a `SyntaxError` for invalid `new import.*(...)` forms. | | `.changeset/new-import-call-syntax-error.md` | Patch release note for rejecting `new import.defer/source(...)` at parse time. | | `.changeset/dynamic-import-sequence-expression.md` | Patch release note for resolving static specifiers from side-effect-free sequence expressions in `import()`. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. body: The heuristic `!/\(/.test(between)` treats any `(` character between `new` and `import` as evidence of parenthesization, but `between` can include comments. This allows invalid code like `new /* ( */ import.defer(...)` to bypass the SyntaxError check. Consider stripping/skipping comments/whitespace after the `new` keyword and only accepting an actual `(` token (outside comments) as the parenthesized form.
