body: The PR title/description focus on test262 known-bugs list cleanup, but this PR also changes parsing/evaluation behavior in `JavascriptParser` and adds two changesets for user-facing fixes. Please update the PR title and/or description to reflect the parser bugfixes and the release-notes impact so reviewers know this is more than a test/comment-only change.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This `SequenceExpression` evaluator assumes `expr.range`/`item.range` are present and indexes into them. However `JavascriptParser.evaluate()` parses with default options (no `ranges: true`), so evaluating a sequence expression there will throw and be surfaced as a `console.warn` from `evaluateExpression()`'s catch block. Consider guarding on missing `range` (or using `start`/`end`) to avoid noisy warnings when `parser.evaluate()` is used (e.g. via DefinePlugin).

author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The new `SequenceExpression` evaluator folds `(a, b, c)` to `c` when the leading expressions are pure. This changes how webpack interprets sequence expressions in call positions: e.g. `(0, eval)("code")` (indirect eval) would now evaluate to identifier `eval`, triggering `parser.hooks.call.for("eval")` and other identifier-based call hooks, which can change bundling semantics. Consider restricting this folding to cases where the tail evaluates to a compile-time value needed for dependency extraction (e.g. string literals / template strings for `import()`/`require()` specifiers), or moving the sequence-handling to the specific import/require evaluation path so indirect-call patterns don’t get reclassified as direct calls.

Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **test/configCases/css/css-loader/test.config.js:13** * `link.href` is hard-coded to `bundle0.css`, which works only as long as the `link` config stays at index 0 (so the emitted CSS asset remains `bundle0.css`). Since this test now runs multiple configs in an array, consider deriving the CSS filename from `options.output.filename` (e.g., swap the extension to `.css`) to make the test resilient to reordering or filename changes. ``` if (options.name !== "link") return; const link = scope.window.document.createElement("link"); link.rel = "stylesheet"; link.href = "bundle0.css"; scope.window.document.head.appendChild(link); ```

## Pull request overview This PR extends webpack’s CSS Modules parsing so locally-defined `@value` identifiers can be used as module requests in `@import` and inside `url()`, allowing those references to be resolved via webpack’s normal resolver/asset pipeline. **Changes:** - Teach the CSS tokenizer/parser to recognize `@import ;` and resolve it via CSS Modules `@value` (local-only), emitting warnings for unsupported imported `@value`s. - Add CSS Modules `url()` handling to resolve `url()` via local `@value` definitions (with warnings for imported `@value`s). - Add a new `configCases` test suite (including a fixture image) plus a changeset documenting the new feature. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 10 out of 11 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | lib/css/walkCssTokens.js | Extends `@import` tokenization to record when the URL was provided as an identifier. | | lib/css/CssParser.js | Resolves `@import` and `url()` identifier forms via local CSS Modules `@value` definitions and emits warnings for unsupported cases. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-url-import/webpack.config.js | Adds a config case enabling CSS experiments and CSS Modules for the new scenario. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-url-import/style.module.css | Exercises local `@value` usage in `@import` and `url()` (quoted and bare forms). | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-url-import/values.module.css | Provides exported `@value` definitions used by the warning scenario. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-url-import/imported.module.css | Simple imported stylesheet content used to assert `@import` worked. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-url-import/imported-via-value.module.css | Exercises warning paths for imported `@value` used in `@import` and `url()`. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-url-import/index.js | Asserts behavior for resolved `@import`/`url()` and warning-only cases. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-url-import/warnings.js | Asserts the expected warnings are emitted for unsupported imported `@value` positions. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-url-import/img/shape.png | Fixture asset referenced by `@value`-backed `url()` cases. | | .changeset/css-modules-value-url-import.md | Declares a minor release note for the new CSS Modules capability. |

body: In the `@import` identifier path, the check `if (!def.value)` treats locally-defined `@value` entries with an empty string value (e.g. `@value p: ;` or `@value p: "";` after trimming) as if they were imported, which triggers the wrong warning/behavior. Use an explicit `def.value === undefined` (or `def.value !== undefined`) check like the `url()` handling below to distinguish imported values from local definitions.

body: Renaming this BuildMeta flag can break third-party plugins/loaders that still read/write `buildMeta.isCSSModule`. Consider keeping `isCSSModule` as a deprecated alias in the JSDoc (and thus in generated typings) for at least one major version, so consumers can migrate without runtime breakage.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Only `isCssModule` is set here. To preserve backward compatibility for plugins/loaders (and potentially persisted artifacts) that still set/read `isCSSModule`, consider also setting the deprecated `buildMeta.isCSSModule` alias (or otherwise ensuring both names stay in sync during a transition).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This condition now only checks `buildMeta.isCssModule`. For compatibility with modules produced by third-party tooling that still sets `buildMeta.isCSSModule`, consider checking both (e.g. new name with fallback to the old name) so CSS modules still get stable IDs during the transition.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `isCssModule` is read directly from buildMeta; if a module still has the legacy `isCSSModule` flag (e.g. from third-party tooling), this will evaluate to undefined/false and change codegen behavior. Consider reading `isCssModule` with a fallback to `isCSSModule` during a deprecation period.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This changeset is marked as a patch, but renaming a runtime-visible property like `module.buildMeta.isCSSModule` can be a breaking change for ecosystem plugins. Either add a compatibility/deprecation path (support both names temporarily) or consider bumping the changeset to at least a minor release.

## Pull request overview Adds a new callback-driven HTML tokenizer intended as the core scanning primitive for the `experiments.html` feature, along with unit tests and fixture-based snapshots to validate tokenization behavior. **Changes:** - Added `lib/html/walkHtmlTokens.js`: single-pass tokenizer emitting open/close tags, attributes, comments, and text via callbacks. - Added `test/walkHtmlTokens.unittest.js` plus HTML fixtures to snapshot token streams and verify roundtrip reconstruction. - Added Jest snapshot output for the fixture corpus. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 8 out of 8 changed files in this pull request and generated 5 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | lib/html/walkHtmlTokens.js | Introduces the core HTML tokenizer and exports quote-type constants. | | test/walkHtmlTokens.unittest.js | Adds unit tests for tokenization, snapshots, and roundtrip reconstruction. | | test/__snapshots__/walkHtmlTokens.unittest.js.snap | Stores snapshot token streams for the HTML fixture corpus. | | test/fixtures/html/parsing/cases/basic.html | Fixture covering common HTML structures, attributes, and comments. | | test/fixtures/html/parsing/cases/attributes.html | Fixture focused on quote types and attribute edge cases. | | test/fixtures/html/parsing/cases/comments.html | Fixture focused on single/multiline/empty HTML comments. | | test/fixtures/html/parsing/cases/nested.html | Fixture covering nested tags and mixed text content. | | test/fixtures/html/parsing/cases/self-closing.html | Fixture covering void/self-closing-ish tag forms. |

author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `module.exports` doesn't provide defaults for `pos`/`callbacks`. If a caller follows the `walkCssTokens` pattern and calls `walkHtmlTokens(input)` or `walkHtmlTokens(input, 0)`, this will throw when accessing `callbacks.text` (or return `undefined` when `pos` is missing). Consider defaulting to `pos = 0` and `callbacks = {}` to make the API robust and consistent with the CSS tokenizer.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The final text flush ignores the return value of `callbacks.text`, but earlier flushes assign `pos = callbacks.text(...)`. For consistency (and to honor the documented “callbacks return new position” contract), this should update `pos` with the callback result.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: In the `</tag>` branch, `closeTag` can be emitted even when no tag name exists (e.g. `</>` results in `nameStart === nameEnd`, so consumers see an empty tag name). Consider requiring at least one valid name character (e.g. ASCII alpha at `nameStart`) before treating this as a close tag; otherwise fall back to treating it as text.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `_consumeUnquotedValue` only stops on whitespace or `>`. If an unquoted attribute value contains a `<` (common in malformed/templated HTML), this function will consume across what should be the start of a new tag and can desynchronize tokenization. Consider also terminating on `<` (and possibly quotes) to better match HTML tokenization behavior and improve resilience.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This comment says the snapshot excludes raw text, but the test snapshots currently include many `text` tokens. Either update the comment to match behavior, or filter out `text` tokens before snapshotting if that’s the intent.

body: `moduleScope` is invoked multiple times by the test harness (once during runner setup and again for each bundle/module execution). As written, it appends a new `<link href="bundle0.css">` on every invocation, which leads to duplicate stylesheet links and an inflated snapshot (the same CSS repeated many times). Make `moduleScope` idempotent (e.g., guard with a module-level `once` flag, or check existing `link[href$="bundle0.css"]` before appending) so only one link is added.

## Pull request overview This PR tightens CSS Modules behavior to avoid creating duplicate module instances when the same `.module.` file is reached both directly (JS import) and indirectly via ICSS (`composes from` / `:import`), and adds a dedicated circular-`composes` fixture to pin self-referential behavior against css-loader. **Changes:** - Prevent duplicate CSS module instances by excluding `.module.` from the `css-import-(local|global)-module` default rules (so `.module.*` continues to be classified by the `css/auto` rule). - Collapse `composes: from "./self.module.css"` inside `self.module.css` to a self-reference (and treat exact same-name self-compose as a no-op). - Update/extend config-case tests and snapshots (including a new `composes-circular.module.css` fixture) and adjust css-modules config-case bundling expectations for deterministic IDs. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 14 out of 14 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | test/configCases/css/reexport/__snapshots__/ConfigTest.snap | Snapshot updates reflecting deduped CSS module output/class exports. | | test/configCases/css/reexport/__snapshots__/ConfigCacheTest.snap | Cache snapshot updates reflecting deduped CSS module output/class exports. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules/webpack.config.js | Narrows parser-option overrides to the entry stylesheet via a targeted rule. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules/warnings.js | Updates expected warning ordering/occurrence for css-modules config cases. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules/test.config.js | Makes bundle selection robust to deterministic numeric chunk prefixes. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules/index.js | Reads the production CSS output using a directory scan for the deterministic chunk name. | | test/configCases/css/css-loader/index.js | Adds the new circular composes fixture to the css-loader parity test list. | | test/configCases/css/css-loader/composes-circular.module.css | New fixture covering self-referential `composes from` in the same file. | | test/configCases/css/css-loader/__snapshots__/ConfigTest.snap | Snapshot updates + new snapshot entry for the circular composes fixture across export types. | | test/configCases/css/css-loader/__snapshots__/ConfigCacheTest.snap | Cache snapshot updates + new snapshot entry for the circular composes fixture. | | lib/css/CssParser.js | Adds self-reference detection for `composes from` and collapses same-file requests to self-references. | | lib/config/defaults.js | Excludes `.module.` from `css-import-(local|global)-module` default rules to avoid duplicate module instances. | | .changeset/css-modules-dedup-icss-import.md | Changeset documenting the dedup fix and parser-option side effect. | | .changeset/css-composes-circular-self-reference.md | Changeset documenting the self-referential `composes from` fix. |

body: `isSelfReferenceRequest` compares only `parseResource(...).path`, ignoring `query`/`fragment`. This can incorrectly collapse `composes: ... from "./self.module.css?foo"` (or modules loaded with a resource query) into a self-reference even though Webpack would treat the queried resource as a distinct module. Consider including `query`/`fragment` in the equality check (compare parsed `{path, query, fragment}` for both) or explicitly returning `false` when the request/module has a query/fragment so the normal import path is used.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: In the production branch, `files.find(...)` can return `undefined`, which then gets passed to `path.join`/`readFileSync` and fails with a less-informative error. Consider adding an explicit check and throwing a clearer error (or using the existing deterministic-bundle helper pattern) when the expected `*.bundle1.css` file isn't found.

body: LocConverter's backwards seek branch computes `this.column = pos - i`, where `i` is the index of the previous newline (or -1). This makes `column` off-by-one compared to the forward path (e.g. when `i === -1`, column becomes `pos + 1`). Consider adjusting the calculation to keep column consistently 0-based (e.g. subtract 1 when `i !== -1`, and handle `i === -1` as `column = pos`).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `dep.setLoc(...)` is currently based on the whole attribute value range (`start..end`) rather than the actual URL candidate range inside `srcset`/`imagesrcset`. This makes locations imprecise (and also forces LocConverter to seek backwards on the next candidate). Consider setting loc from `start + innerStart` to `start + innerEnd` for each parsed URL.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: PR description says `link` handling will be done in a follow-up, but this parser already processes `<link>` (e.g. `href`/`imagesrcset` via `DEFAULT_SOURCES`). Either update the PR description or defer `<link>` handling to match the stated scope.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The inner template class is named `CssUrlDependencyTemplate`, which looks like a copy/paste artifact and is confusing for HTML dependencies. Renaming it to something like `HtmlSourceDependencyTemplate` would improve clarity (especially when debugging dependencyTemplates).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `HtmlSourceDependency` is made serializable with key `webpack/lib/dependencies/HtmlSourceDependency`, but there is no corresponding loader entry in `lib/util/internalSerializables.js`. This will trigger `... not found in internalSerializables` warnings and can break deserialization when webpack is bundled. Add a `dependencies/HtmlSourceDependency` entry that requires this module (similar to `dependencies/CssUrlDependency`).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `moduleScope` is invoked multiple times by the test runner (initial setup + again per executed CJS module). As written, this appends a new `<link>` each time, which can lead to duplicate stylesheet loads and snapshot flakiness. Make this setup idempotent (e.g. reuse an existing link for the same `href`, or remove/replace prior links).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This filter disables the cache variant of the test suite (`ConfigCacheTestCases`). Since HTML parsing/generation is cache-sensitive (serialization/deserialization), it would be better to cover the cached run too. If there is a known cache-specific issue, consider asserting it explicitly instead of skipping cache entirely.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This changeset text still frames `experiments.html` as only reserving the `html` module type, but this PR also adds a real parser/generator and URL dependency handling for HTML modules. Consider updating the changeset to reflect the actual user-visible behavior added here (importing HTML as a module and rewriting asset references).

This makes `getSize` return 0 for HTML modules, which can skew optimizations (e.g. concatenation heuristics). Consider estimating size from `module.originalSource()` (or store the string length in buildInfo again).

## Pull request overview Improves webpack’s `JavascriptParser.isPure` so PURE-annotation scanning doesn’t leak across AST boundaries (notably between `ObjectExpression` properties) and expands purity handling to additional expression forms, improving tree-shaking correctness. **Changes:** - Fix `ObjectExpression` purity evaluation by advancing `commentsStartPos` to the end of each full property range (prevents PURE comment leakage into later computed keys). - Extend `isPure` support for `ChainExpression`, selected `UnaryExpression` operators, `MetaProperty`, strict `BinaryExpression` equality, and `TaggedTemplateExpression`; also thread comment scanning through `TemplateLiteral` interpolations. - Add new inner-graph config cases covering the regression and the newly supported expression types; update changeset text accordingly. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 6 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/javascript/JavascriptParser.js` | Adjusts PURE comment range tracking and expands `isPure` handling for more AST node types. | | `test/configCases/inner-graph/pure-expressions-object-leak/module.js` | Regression case ensuring PURE annotations don’t leak across object properties. | | `test/configCases/inner-graph/pure-expressions-object-leak/webpack.config.js` | Test harness config for the object-property PURE leak scenario. | | `test/configCases/inner-graph/pure-expressions-extended/module.js` | Adds coverage for additional expression types (chain, unary, tagged template, strict equality, template interpolation PURE). | | `test/configCases/inner-graph/pure-expressions-extended/webpack.config.js` | Test harness config for the extended `isPure` cases. | | `.changeset/ispure-more-expression-types.md` | Updates release note describing the expanded `isPure` coverage. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

body: `TaggedTemplateExpression` duplicates the `__PURE__` detection regexp; `CallExpression` already uses `CompilerHintNotationRegExp.Pure`. Reusing the shared regexp here would avoid divergence if the notation changes later.

Consider mentioning these behavior changes as well so the release note matches the full tree-shaking impact.

body: `a?.()` uses optional chaining syntax, which is not supported by Node.js 10.x (still in the integration test matrix). Because configCases bundles are parsed by the Node runtime, this will cause a SyntaxError on Node 10 even if the code path is never executed. Please avoid optional chaining in integration configCases (e.g. move ChainExpression coverage to a unit test that runs on modern Node, or restructure the case so the optional-chaining code is guaranteed to be removed from emitted bundles on Node 10).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The comment claims "`void` and `!` coerce via ToBoolean", but `void` does not coerce its operand; it evaluates the operand and returns `undefined`. Please adjust this comment to match JS semantics so future changes to `isPure` aren’t based on incorrect assumptions.

body: The PR title/description focus on the ObjectExpression PURE-comment leak (and TemplateLiteral comment scanning), but this changeset indicates broader behavior changes (new expression types recognized as pure). Please update the PR title/description to reflect the broader scope, or split the additional expression-type support into a separate PR/changeset for easier review and release notes accuracy.

body: In the `TemplateLiteral` purity check, threading the caller-provided `commentsStartPos` into the first interpolation broadens the scanned comment range to include comments that appear *before the template literal itself* (e.g. `key: /*#__PURE__*/ `x${fn()}`), which can incorrectly make the first `${...}` expression look PURE. Consider starting a local cursor at the template literal start (`expr.range[0]`) and threading that through interpolations instead, so only comments within the template literal contribute.

## Pull request overview This PR updates webpack’s default resolver `conditionNames` to include Node.js’s `"module-sync"` export condition, aiming to better align package `exports` resolution behavior with modern Node.js for synchronously-loadable ESM. It also adds coverage to validate the behavior and ensures the test runner environment resolves `exports` using the same condition set. **Changes:** - Add `"module-sync"` to default resolver `conditionNames` in `getResolveDefaults` (and inherited dependency-type defaults like worker). - Add `"module-sync"` to `FileSystemInfo` build-dependency resolvers’ `conditionNames`. - Add a new resolving test case/fixtures for `"module-sync"`, update defaults snapshots, and adjust Jest/ESLint config to support the new test setup. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 8 out of 22 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/config/defaults.js` | Adds `"module-sync"` to default CJS/ESM dependency resolver condition names. | | `lib/FileSystemInfo.js` | Adds `"module-sync"` to build dependency resolution conditions (CJS + ESM). | | `test/Defaults.unittest.js` | Updates snapshots to reflect the new default `conditionNames` ordering. | | `test/cases/resolving/module-sync/test.filter.js` | Skips the resolving case when the running Node doesn’t activate `"module-sync"` for `require()`. | | `test/cases/resolving/module-sync/test.config.js` | Injects Node `require`/dynamic `import()` helpers into the test runtime for comparison. | | `test/cases/resolving/module-sync/index.js` | Verifies webpack resolution matches Node behavior across different `exports` condition orderings. | | `test/cases/resolving/module-sync/node_modules/**` | Adds fixture packages exercising `"module-sync"` vs `import`/`require`/`default` branches. | | `jest.config.js` | Adds `testEnvironmentOptions.customExportConditions` to include `"module-sync"` for Jest’s resolver. | | `eslint.config.mjs` | Adds an override allowing dynamic `import()` syntax in `test.config.js` under `test/cases/**`. | | `.changeset/module-sync-condition.md` | Declares a minor release note for the resolver default change. |
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The ESLint override enables parsing ES2020 and ignores the `dynamic-import` syntax rule for *all* `test/cases/**/test.config.js` files, but the only new need appears to be the module-sync fixture. Consider narrowing `files` to the specific fixture (or a smaller glob) so other test configs keep stricter linting against unsupported syntax.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Good catch — the ESLint override has been removed entirely in 7b7525a. Setting `customExportConditions` to include `"module-sync"` globally turned out to break unrelated tests (e.g. `configCases/module/custom-javascript-parser` failing with "Must use import to load ES Module" because real deps like `meriyah`/`file-type` point their `module-sync` exports at `.mjs` files that Jest's CJS runtime can't load). Reverted both the jest config change and the ESLint override, and went back to the isolated child-process probe in `test/cases/resolving/module-sync/test.config.js` — no dynamic-import in test configs, no global lint relaxation.

body: `ModuleExternalInitFragment` only prefixes `defer` for namespace imports (`imported === true`). For `import defer` with default/named/side-effect-only imports, the generated statement currently drops the phase keyword (falls back to plain `import …`). The phase keyword should be applied consistently across all supported static import forms (or explicitly rejected earlier if some forms aren’t meant to be supported).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `ExternalModule` instances are de-duplicated in the compilation by `module.identifier()`. Since phase/attributes are stored on `this.dependencyMeta` and now affect emitted import statements, two imports of the same external with different phases can collapse to a single module and make the emitted phase depend on whichever dependency was seen first. Consider incorporating relevant `dependencyMeta` (at least `phase`, and likely `attributes`) into `identifier()` (and `updateHash`) so differing import metadata doesn’t get silently merged.

## Pull request overview This PR fixes long-term caching correctness for CSS emitted via `experiments.css` by ensuring CSS chunk hashing and cached code-generation output are invalidated when the final, hashed filename of an asset referenced from CSS changes (e.g. `url("./logo.png")` pointing to `logo..png` after rebuild). **Changes:** - Include the referenced asset module’s build hash in `CssUrlDependency.updateHash` so CSS module/chunk `[contenthash]` changes when the asset content changes. - Fix `AssetGenerator.generate` URL metadata merging so freshly computed URLs are not overwritten by persisted `data.url` entries from previous builds. - Add a watch test case reproducing the stale CSS filename and stale `url(...)` content failure modes, and update affected snapshot expectations. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 10 out of 12 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.

body: The comment describing the GC/microtask drain sequence doesn’t match what the code does. It says “GC -> microtask drain -> GC, repeated”, but the loop performs only one GC per iteration (then a final GC after setImmediate). Update the comment to reflect the actual sequence, or add the second GC if that was intended.

body: The docstring for `primeTaskAsync` says it runs a task’s “full lifecycle once”, but the implementation only invokes the task hooks (beforeAll/afterAll via `meta.options`) plus a single `iterationAsync` call; it doesn’t run the bench-level setup/teardown (and also doesn’t start/stop `mongoMeasurement`). Consider clarifying the comment to match what’s actually executed, or include the missing lifecycle pieces if they’re required for priming.

body: PR description mentions mirroring the 4/4 sharding used by the `benchmark` job so each shard runs in a fresh Node process. The `benchmark-memory` job is still unsharded (no matrix and no `SHARD` env), so memory mode will continue to execute all benchmarks in one process. If sharding is intended, add a shard matrix and pass `SHARD: ${{ matrix.shard }}` (like the `benchmark` job).

## Pull request overview This PR fixes a correctness issue in Webpack’s CSS Modules `@value` handling: when the same local `@value` name is imported from multiple modules in the same file, references are now resolved against the import that was in scope at the reference position (source-order), rather than always resolving via the first matching import dependency. **Changes:** - Track the active `@value` import request at parse-time and propagate it through ICSS symbol/export dependency resolution. - Update ICSS export resolution to select the matching `CssIcssImportDependency` by both `localName` and `request` (with fallback). - Add a new config-case test suite covering local overrides, import overrides, aliasing, and chained re-aliasing scenarios. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 9 out of 9 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-overriding/webpack.config.js | Adds a new css/module config-case to exercise `@value` override behaviors. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-overriding/style.module.css | Defines ordered local/imported `@value` scenarios, including same-name multi-import cases. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-overriding/index.js | Asserts generated CSS substitutions and exported class renaming for each scenario. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-overriding/colors-a.module.css | Provides imported `@value` definitions for the new test. | | test/configCases/css/css-modules-value-overriding/colors-b.module.css | Provides a second imported `@value` source to validate same-name import override. | | lib/dependencies/CssIcssSymbolDependency.js | Adds `request` to disambiguate same-name imports during symbol replacement and hashing/serialization. | | lib/dependencies/CssIcssExportDependency.js | Extends reference tuples to optionally carry `request` and resolves imports using a `localName`+`request` lookup. | | lib/css/CssParser.js | Records the active `@value` import `request` in ICSS definitions and emits it on symbol/export deps. | | .changeset/css-value-import-source-order.md | Documents the fix as a patch-level change. |

## Pull request overview This PR introduces a new `parser.pure` option for `css/module` and `css/auto` module types to enforce “pure selector” semantics (every selector must include at least one local class or id), aligning webpack’s built-in CSS Modules behavior with `postcss-modules-local-by-default` pure mode. **Changes:** - Add `pure` parser option support in `lib/css/CssParser.js`, including error emission and comment-based opt-outs (`cssmodules-pure-ignore`, `cssmodules-pure-no-check`). - Extend public typings and JSON schemas to expose/validate the new option for `css/module` and `css/auto`. - Add/adjust config-case tests to cover pure-mode behavior and expected errors. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 18 out of 22 changed files in this pull request and generated 4 comments.

`lib/css/CssModulesPlugin.js` | Switches `css/module` + `css/auto` parser option validation to the new schema/type that includes `pure`. | | `schemas/WebpackOptions.json` | Adds `CssParserPure` and `CssAutoOrModuleParserOptions` schema definitions; wires them into parser options by module type. | | `schemas/plugins/css/CssAutoOrModuleParserOptions.json` | Adds plugin schema entry for the new parser-options shape. | | `schemas/plugins/css/CssAutoOrModuleParserOptions.check.js` | Generated runtime validator for the new schema. | | `schemas/plugins/css/CssAutoOrModuleParserOptions.check.d.ts` | Typings for the generated validator. | | `declarations/WebpackOptions.d.ts` | Adds `CssAutoOrModuleParserOptions` + `CssParserPure` to test case enabling `parser.pure` for `css/module`. | | `test/configCases/css/pure-parser-options/index.js` | Asserts locals exports for pure-compliant modules and file-level no-check behavior. | | Fixture for file-leading `cssmodules-pure-no-check`. | | `test/configCases/css/pure-parser-options/invalid.module.css` | Fixture containing impure selectors expected to error. | | `test/configCases/css/pure-parser-options/errors.js` | Expected error patterns for the new config case. | | `test/configCases/css/postcss-modules-plugins/webpack.config.js` | Enables `parser.pure` for the existing “pure” fixture in this config case. | | `test/configCases/css/postcss-modules-plugins/postcss-modules-local-by-default.pure.modules.css` | Removes an outdated “TODO not implemented yet” `test/configCases/css/postcss-modules-plugins/__snapshots__/ConfigTest.snap` | Snapshot update reflecting removal of the TODO comment. | | author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `ancestorHadLocal` is currently derived from `parentEffectivePure() || lastSelectorHadLocal`, where `lastSelectorHadLocal` only reflects the last selector in a selector list. In pure mode this can incorrectly treat nested rules as pure when the parent prelude contains a mixed selector list (e.g. `:global(.a), .b { span { ... } }`), because a single trailing local selector makes `parentEffectivePure()` true for all nested selectors even though some expanded selectors remain global-only. Consider tracking/propagating parent *purity* (all selectors pure) instead of “has any local”, and incorporate `currentRuleHasImpureSelector` when computing the value propagated to children / `&` resolution.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `currentRulePreludeStart` is only updated on `{`/`}`. If a top-level at-rule ends with `;` (e.g. `@import ...;`) before an impure selector, `reportPureRule()` will slice from the previous `}`/0 and can include the preceding at-rule text in the reported selector and loc range. Consider updating `currentRulePreludeStart` on top-level semicolons (and potentially other top-level boundaries) so the prelude slice starts at the actual selector.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `seenTopLevelRule` is only set when a top-level `{` is parsed. This means `/* cssmodules-pure-no-check */` can still be accepted after top-level at-rules that end with `;` (e.g. `@import`, `@charset`), even though the changeset says it must appear among the leading comments before any rule. Consider marking `seenTopLevelRule` on any top-level rule/at-rule (including `;`-terminated at-rules) so `pureNoCheck` can’t be enabled after code has started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Pure-mode bookkeeping (stack push/pop, selector tracking, etc.) currently runs for all CSS modules parses, even when `parser.pure` is not enabled (`pureMode` is false). Since CSS parsing is performance-sensitive, consider guarding most of this logic behind `pureMode` (e.g. don’t maintain `pureBlockStack` / `currentSelectorHasLocal` unless needed) to avoid adding overhead to the default path.

## Pull request overview Adds a new `linkInsert` compilation hook to Webpack’s CSS loading runtime so plugin authors can customize how stylesheet `` tags are attached to the document at runtime. **Changes:** - Introduces `linkInsert` as a new `SyncWaterfallHook` on `CssLoadingRuntimeModule.getCompilationHooks(compilation)`. - Updates the generated CSS loading runtime to call `linkInsert` (defaulting to `document.head.appendChild(link);`) when attaching newly created stylesheet links. - Adds a new config case to validate the hook can be tapped, plus a changeset describing the new capability. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 6 out of 7 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | lib/css/CssLoadingRuntimeModule.js | Defines the new hook and applies it to the link-attachment logic in the CSS loading runtime. | | types.d.ts | Updates the generated type surface to include `linkInsert` in `CssLoadingRuntimeModulePluginHooks`. | | .changeset/css-loading-link-insert-hook.md | Declares a minor release and documents the new hook’s intent and signature. | | test/configCases/css/link-insert-hook/webpack.config.js | Adds a config case plugin that taps `linkInsert` to customize insertion behavior. | | test/configCases/css/link-insert-hook/index.js | Adds an assertion that the hook’s custom code ran (via a custom attribute). | | test/configCases/css/link-insert-hook/test.config.js | Ensures the correct bundles are executed for the new config case. | | test/configCases/css/link-insert-hook/style.css | Adds a simple CSS file to trigger stylesheet chunk loading. |

body: `linkInsert` is only applied for the non-HMR insertion path (`needAttach` branch). When `hmr` is truthy, insertion is hard-coded to `document.head.insertBefore(link, hmr)`, so plugin authors cannot control link placement in HMR updates even though the new hook suggests it controls insertion. Consider applying the hook to the HMR insertion path as well (passing the default `insertBefore` source), or explicitly documenting that HMR insert behavior is not customizable.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This test currently only asserts that the hook ran (via the custom attribute), but it doesn’t verify the core behavior claimed by the hook: controlling *where* the `<link>` is inserted. Consider adding a sentinel element to `document.head` before importing the CSS and asserting the stylesheet link is inserted before it (or is `document.head.firstChild`).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The changeset says plugin developers can control where stylesheet `<link>` elements are inserted, but the implementation still uses a fixed `insertBefore(link, hmr)` when HMR passes an anchor element. Either clarify this limitation here or update the implementation so `linkInsert` can also override the HMR insertion path.

body: The `sourceOrder` “bumping” logic only moves the current file’s import deps forward, but it does not propagate that shift to files that were previously required to come after it. This can violate earlier per-rule constraints even when the overall constraint graph is acyclic (e.g. rule1 enforces A < B, later rule enforces C < A; bumping A past C will leave B behind and break A < B). This needs a true topological sort (or equivalent constraint propagation over outgoing edges) across all composed files, with deterministic tie-breaking and cycle handling.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This changeset describes reordering via “topological sort (Kahn's algorithm), falling back to source order on a cycle”, but the implementation in `CssParser` currently uses an incremental `sourceOrder` bumping approach and does not implement Kahn’s algorithm or a cycle fallback. Please update the release note (and/or implementation) so the documented behavior matches what ships, especially around cycle handling/determinism.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `testConfig.moduleScope` can be invoked multiple times by the test runner (it’s called during runner setup and again for each executed module). This implementation unconditionally appends a new `<link>` each time, which can change link ordering and make tests flaky. Consider guarding so the link is only added once (e.g. check for an existing link with the same href or use a module-level `once` flag).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Selecting `document.getElementsByTagName("link")[1]` is brittle when additional `<link>` elements may be present (or appended multiple times if `moduleScope` runs more than once). Prefer locating the stylesheet link by `href` (e.g. match `bundle0.css`) or by a unique id/data-attribute set in `test.config.js`.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 16 out of 16 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `ready.sort(bySource)` is executed on every iteration of the Kahn loop, making this implementation O(n^2 log n) in the number of nodes in the worst case. Since this runs during CSS parsing, it may become noticeable on large graphs. Consider maintaining `ready` as a priority queue (min-heap by `sourceIndex`) or keeping it sorted via binary insertion so each node selection is O(log n).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `topologicalSort` implicitly assumes that every node referenced in `graph` is also present in the `nodes` array. If that assumption is violated, `inDegree.get(to)` / `sourceIndex.get(a)` can return `undefined`, leading to `NaN` degrees and unstable sorting. Consider either validating this assumption up front (and throwing / early return with a clear error) or defensively skipping edges/nodes that aren't part of the provided `nodes` set.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 16 out of 16 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

body: The complexity note is inaccurate. With the linear scan to pick the smallest-source-index node from `ready` each iteration, the runtime is O(V^2 + E) (or O(V^2) when E≈V), not O(V·(V + E)). Consider updating the comment to avoid misleading future callers (or switching to a heap if you want O((V+E) log V)).

body: ## Pull request overview Adds HTML-module support for treating external module references as build-time dependencies, specifically bundling `
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: For classic `<script src>` entries, `HtmlScriptSrcDependency` is constructed without a category, so it falls back to `"javascript/script"`. That value doesn’t exist in the default `resolve.byDependency` map, so these requests won’t get the intended CommonJS/ESM resolve settings (mainFields/conditionNames/extensions), and package/extensionless requests can resolve differently than expected. Consider using an existing dependency type (e.g. `"commonjs"` for classic scripts and `"esm"` for module scripts) and/or defaulting to `"commonjs"` here instead of `"javascript/script"`.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The default dependency category `"javascript/script"` is not a standard `resolve.byDependency` key (defaults include e.g. `esm`, `commonjs`, `url`, `worker`, `unknown`). When this dependency is created without an explicit category, it will bypass the per-dependency resolve defaults (mainFields/conditionNames/extensions), which can break resolution for package and extensionless script-src requests. Please default this to an existing dependency type (likely `"commonjs"`) and only use `"esm"` when `<script type="module">`.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 38 out of 40 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
The `dependOn` calculation currently includes *all* previously-seen entries in the group (`dependOn: [...previousNames]`). For ESM output this causes later entry chunks to import/register (and therefore evaluate) those earlier entry chunks (see modulepreload-esm snapshot where the final entry chunk imports chunks 1/2 in addition to the runtime). That means `<link rel="modulepreload">` entries can end up executing when a later `<script type="module">` runs, which breaks the expected "preload without execution" semantics and can introduce unintended side effects.

Consider making each entry depend only on the group leader (the first entry name) to share the runtime without forcing evaluation of other prior entries (i.e., keep a single `runtimeEntryName` per group and set `dependOn` to that for subsequent entries).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 39 out of 41 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The entry-name prefix is hashed from `module.rawRequest` (e.g. `./page.html`), which is not guaranteed to be unique across different HTML modules (two modules imported as `./page.html` from different directories can collide). This can lead to duplicate `__html_<hash>_<idx>` entry names and conflicting entries/chunks. Consider hashing a unique identifier instead (e.g. `module.identifier()` or `module.resource` + `module.resourceResolveData`), even if it’s less “stable across machines`.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `finishMake` picks the first entry in each group as the `dependOn` leader. When the first ESM-group item is a `<link rel="modulepreload">`, later `<script type="module" src>` entries will import that leader to reuse the runtime, which executes the leader entry module and can violate the “modulepreload must preload without running” behavior for preload-only modules that are not otherwise imported. Consider separating modulepreload into its own non-leader group, or selecting a leader that is guaranteed to be an executing script entry (or a dedicated runtime-only leader).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

## Pull request overview

Copilot reviewed 39 out of 41 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `<script src>` is currently treated as an entry unconditionally (`entry: true`). This will also bundle/rewrite non-JavaScript script resources (e.g. `<script type="application/ld+json" src=...>` or other custom types) which browsers do not execute as JS, changing semantics and potentially breaking pages. Consider making `entry` conditional on the script being executable JS (no `type`, `type="module"`, or known JS MIME types like `text/javascript` / `application/javascript`) and otherwise fall back to the existing `HtmlSourceDependency` behavior.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: When `experiments.outputModule` is enabled, `output.module` defaults to `true`, so the emitted entry chunks use ESM syntax. For rewritten classic `<script src>` tags (no `type="module"`), the browser will parse them as classic scripts and fail on `export`/`import` syntax in the chunk. The implementation currently rewrites only the `src` URL, leaving the `type` attribute unchanged. Please ensure that, when `compilation.outputOptions.module` (or `outputOptions.scriptType === "module"`) is enabled, rewritten script tags are also updated to `type="module"` (or alternatively avoid generating module-format chunks for classic scripts).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The `comment` handler assumes every token passed here begins with `<!--` and strips a fixed 4‑char prefix. However `walkHtmlTokens` also reports “bogus comments” (e.g. `<!DOCTYPE ...>` / `<?...>`), so this can slice the wrong content and potentially treat non-HTML-comment markup as magic-comment input. Consider first checking the raw prefix is actually `<!--` (and otherwise just reset `pendingWebpackIgnore` and return) before attempting to strip/parse magic comments.

body: ## Pull request overview This PR upgrades Webpack’s HTML tokenizer (`lib/html/walkHtmlTokens.js`) to recognize and tokenize `` using a dedicated, WHATWG-aligned DOCTYPE state machine (mirroring SWC’s lexer), instead of treating DOCTYPE as a generic/bogus comment. It also expands test coverage and updates snapshots to reflect the new token stream. **Changes:** - Add a dedicated DOCTYPE tokenization path and `callbacks.doctype()` emission in `walkHtmlTokens`. - Add CDATA section handling and extend the tokenizer’s terminal EOF flush logic for the new states. - Update unit tests and Jest snapshots to assert DOCTYPE callback behavior and several edge cases. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 4 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/html/walkHtmlTokens.js` | Implements DOCTYPE (and related) tokenizer states, emits `doctype` tokens, and updates EOF flushing logic. | | `test/walkHtmlTokens.unittest.js` | Adds `doctype` callbacks to snapshot/roundtrip harness and adds new focused tests for DOCTYPE/CDATA/EOF behaviors. | | `test/__snapshots__/walkHtmlTokens.unittest.js.snap` | Updates snapshots to reflect DOCTYPE being emitted as `doctype` rather than `comment`. | | `.changeset/html-tokenizer-doctype.md` | Adds a changeset entry describing the tokenizer behavior change. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. body: The newly added COMMENT_LESS_THAN_SIGN* states appear to be unreachable: there is no assignment to `state = STATE_COMMENT_LESS_THAN_SIGN` anywhere in this file. Either wire these states in (per spec, in `STATE_COMMENT` when `cc === CC_LESS_THAN` transition into `STATE_COMMENT_LESS_THAN_SIGN`) or remove the unused states to avoid dead code and confusion about spec compliance.

body: Behavior change: DOCTYPE tokens are now only emitted via `callbacks.doctype`. If a caller still only provides `callbacks.comment` (the previous behavior was to dispatch `<!DOCTYPE …>` through `comment`), the DOCTYPE will be silently skipped and cannot be round-tripped. Consider providing a compatibility fallback (e.g. call `callbacks.doctype` when present, otherwise fall back to `callbacks.comment`), or explicitly document this as a breaking callback-contract change for `walkHtmlTokens` consumers.

## Pull request overview This PR changes how CSS `@import` dependencies are handled for `exportType: "text"` and `exportType: "css-style-sheet"` so that imported CSS is merged at build time (rather than concatenated at runtime). The goal is to produce a single inline source map per generated CSS literal that correctly maps selectors back to their original (including transitively imported) source files. **Changes:** - Inline `@import`ed CSS into the parent module’s generated literal at build time for `text` / `css-style-sheet`, removing the runtime merge helper. - Add compilation scheduling support by registering `CssImportDependency` as a code-generation dependency for `text` / `css-style-sheet` parents. - Add a nested-`@import` sourcemap regression test case and update existing charset snapshots. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 14 out of 15 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment.

body:
## Pull request overview This PR fixes three correctness issues in CSS ICSS dependencies. (1) Spurious "name not found" warnings under `camel-case-only`/`dashes-only` because `getWarnings` in `CssIcssExportDependency` and `CssIcssImportDependency` compared the raw composed/imported name against `ExportsInfo`, which only stores convention-mapped names. (2) `CssIcssExportDependency.updateHash` previously omitted `value`, `range`, `interpolate`, `exportMode`, and `exportType`, so cache invalidation missed changes like `composes: foo` → `composes: bar`. `CssIcssSymbolDependency.updateHash` is similarly hardened with field separators. (3) `getReferencedExports` for `SELF_REFERENCE` returned `this.name` (the composing class) rather than `this.value` (the composed class), now convention-expanded. **Changes:** - Convention-aware export lookup in `getWarnings` for both export and import ICSS deps. - Fix `getReferencedExports` for `SELF_REFERENCE` to use `this.value` expanded through `exportsConvention`. - Strengthen `updateHash` in both `CssIcssExportDependency` and `CssIcssSymbolDependency`; regenerate affected snapshots; add new configCase. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 10 out of 10 changed files in this pull request and generated no comments.
Show a summary per file | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | lib/dependencies/CssIcssExportDependency.js | Convention-aware `getWarnings`/`getReferencedExports`; broader `updateHash`. | | lib/dependencies/CssIcssImportDependency.js | Convention-aware `getWarnings` for ICSS import. | | lib/dependencies/CssIcssSymbolDependency.js | Add explicit field separators in `updateHash` to avoid aliasing. | | test/configCases/css/composes-convention-warning/{webpack.config.js,style.modules.css,other.modules.css,index.js} | New test verifying `camel-case-only` composes scenarios produce no spurious warnings. | | test/configCases/css/local-ident-name/__snapshots__/{ConfigTest,ConfigCacheTest}.snap | Regenerated hashes due to strengthened `updateHash`. | | .changeset/css-icss-exports-convention-and-hash.md | Changeset describing the patch. |

body:
Appropriate RCDATA end tags followed by attributes are sent into the shared attribute states, but those states emit `openTag` on `>` rather than `closeTag`. For example `<title>x</title data-y>` would drop the `x` text and report the closing tag as an opening tag instead of leaving RCDATA.

This issue also appears on line 1655 of the same file.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Appropriate RAWTEXT end tags followed by attributes are sent into the shared attribute states, which always emit `openTag` on `>` instead of `closeTag`. This mis-tokenizes inputs such as `<style>x</style data-y>` and keeps the tokenizer in the wrong content mode.

This issue also appears on line 1778 of the same file.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Appropriate script end tags followed by attributes enter the shared attribute states, but those states emit an opening tag on `>` rather than a closing tag. Inputs like `<script>x</script data-y>` are therefore tokenized incorrectly and the script text can be lost.

This issue also appears on line 1905 of the same file.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Escaped script end tags with attributes enter the shared attribute states, which emit `openTag` on `>` instead of `closeTag`. A sequence such as `<script><!--x</script data-y>` will not close the script token correctly.

This issue also appears on line 2122 of the same file.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The newly added double-escape script states do not appear to have a unit test exercising `<script` / `</script` transitions while already in escaped script data. The existing added script test only covers `<!-- ... -->`, so regressions in this branch would not be caught.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
This builds `tempBuffer` by concatenating every ASCII letter after `<` in escaped script data until a delimiter. A long identifier-like sequence (for example in an inline script comment) can allocate and copy an unbounded string even though the state only needs to know whether the token is `script`.

This issue also appears on line 2315 of the same file.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Skipping content-mode tracking for self-closing start tags leaves non-void special elements such as `<script/>` and `<style/>` in `STATE_DATA`. In HTML these tags are not void, so their following contents should still be tokenized as script/RAWTEXT rather than as normal markup.

body: ## Pull request overview This PR memoizes the resolution of CSS ICSS `:export` / `@value` / `composes` references and the interpolated identifier computation in `CssIcssExportDependency.Template`, using `moduleGraph.cached` keyed on stable inputs. Recursive callers pass an explicit `seen` set and route through new private `_doResolve` / `_doResolveReferences` helpers that bypass the cache to preserve cycle detection. A reported microbenchmark shows 8–24% compile-time wins across three synthetic CSS workloads. **Changes:** - Split `resolve` / `resolveReferences` into a public memoized entry point and a private recursive `_do*` worker; internal recursion now calls the worker directly. - Memoize `getIdentifier`'s interpolation path (`cssExportConvention` + `getLocalIdent` + `escapeIdentifier`) via a module-level `computeInterpolatedIdentifier` keyed on `(module, value, exportType, chunkGraph, runtimeTemplate)`. - Add a changeset describing the optimization. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | lib/dependencies/CssIcssExportDependency.js | Introduces module-level `compute*` helpers and refactors the template's `resolve`, `resolveReferences`, and `getIdentifier` to memoize via `moduleGraph.cached` while keeping recursion uncached. | | .changeset/css-icss-export-resolution-cached.md | Changeset entry for the perf change (currently describes a different implementation than the one shipped). | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.

It claims that "per-module indices of `CssIcssImportDependency` (by local name) and `CssIcssExportDependency` (by export name) are built once per build", and also says "`getLocalIdent` is also memoized per `(module, local)`". However, per the PR description the indexed variant was explicitly not shipped, and the cache key for the interpolated identifier is `(module, value, exportType, chunkGraph, runtimeTemplate)` — not `(module, local)`, and it memoizes the full interpolated identifier (including `cssExportConvention` and `escapeIdentifier`), not just `getLocalIdent`. Please update the changeset text to reflect what's actually implemented.

body: ## Pull request overview This PR reduces `SourceMapDevToolPlugin` memory pressure during source map generation, especially for large builds with many external `.map` assets. **Changes:** - Removes the unused `asset` field from `SourceMapTask`. - Attempts to clear source-map-related source caches after `sourceAndMap()`. - Emits external source map assets as `RawSource(Buffer)` instead of string-backed `RawSource`. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 2 out of 2 changed files in this pull request and generated 1 comment. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `lib/SourceMapDevToolPlugin.js` | Updates source map task data, cache clearing, and `.map` asset serialization. | | `.changeset/source-map-dev-tool-memory.md` | Adds a patch changeset describing the memory reduction. | --- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Sharing the same `WeakSet` across all `clearCache` calls can leave caches behind on shared child sources. Once a source is marked visited during the first asset, later assets that call `sourceAndMap()` can repopulate that source's map/parsed-map caches, but the subsequent `clearCache` traversal skips it because it is already in the shared set. Use a fresh visited set per clear operation (or otherwise allow revisiting already-cleared sources after each new `sourceAndMap()` call) so caches populated by later assets are actually released.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Good catch — that's a real correctness bug. After the first asset's walk added a shared module to the visited set, the next asset's `sourceAndMap()` would repopulate that module's `_cachedMaps`, but the next `clearCache` walk would skip the module and leave the freshly populated caches behind.

Fixed in `bfafb93` by dropping the shared `WeakSet` and letting `webpack-sources` create a fresh one per call. Within-asset recursion still dedupes via the auto-allocated set; cross-asset dedup is gone, but each `Map.clear()` on an already-empty map is O(1), so total work scales with total tree size rather than chunks × shared_modules.

---

author: alexander-akait

body:
Re-measured this carefully — the per-call WeakSet fix in `bfafb93` regresses on both axes:

Same harness (50 chunks × 1000 shared modules, 4 runs averaged):

| variant | peak heap | peak RSS | elapsed |
|---|---|---|---|
| `bfafb93` (per-call) | 1037 MB | **4063 MB** | **23.3 s** |
| `7922bed` (shared WeakSet) | 1030 MB | 3397 MB | 16.4 s |
| Δ | −7 MB | **−666 MB** | **−6.9 s (−30%)** |

The dominant cost isn't the recursion itself — it's that every subsequent chunk's `sourceAndMap()` has to recompute the shared modules whose caches the previous chunk's `clearCache` wiped, churning ~50× more allocations than necessary. Sharing one `visited` set lets each shared subtree be walked once.

The "leak" you flagged is real but bounded — at most one `_cachedMaps` entry per shared module at end of run, which works out to a few MB at the scale of #20961 (5,275 modules × small per-module parsed map). Vs. the +666 MB peak RSS regression of the per-call form, the trade-off goes the wrong way for the OOM scenario this PR is trying to fix.

Restored in `7922bed` with the trade-off documented inline. Thanks for the review — re-running the numbers was useful even though it ended up landing on the original side.

Guarded with `typeof asset.source.clearCache === "function"` in `278696d`.

body:
Agreed — `Buffer.from(str, "utf8")` is variable-width for non-ASCII, and V8 strings are already 1-byte for Latin-1 content, so the "1 byte/char" framing is wrong. The real benefit is moving the bytes to external (off-heap) memory.

Reworded the code comment and the changeset bullet in `278696d` to describe it as "stores the serialized JSON in external memory, reducing V8 heap pressure and exposure to `--max-old-space-size`."

author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `clearCache` is invoked on the current compilation asset (`asset.source`). When `extractSourceAndMap` falls back to the `originalSources` registry (i.e. the current asset is a `RawSource` without a map), the `sourceAndMap()` call that repopulates caches runs on the *pinned original* Source instead — so this call won’t clear the newly-populated `_cachedMaps` / parsed-map caches on that original source tree. Consider having `getTaskForFile`/`extractSourceAndMap` return which `Source` instance actually provided the map (current vs pinned original) and call `clearCache` on that instance.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Good catch — real bug. When `extractSourceAndMap` falls back to the pinned original from `originalSources` (because the current asset is a `RawSource` left by an earlier plugin instance), `sourceAndMap()` runs on the pinned original, populating *its* caches — not the current `asset.source`'s. The previous `clearCache(asset.source, …)` was a no-op for that path.

Fixed in `d1f49e3`: `extractSourceAndMap` now returns the `Source` it actually queried (`mapSource`), it's threaded through `SourceMapTask`, and `clearCache` targets `task.mapSource`.

body: The new comment says the inline data-URL form needs the map as both a string and a buffer and that they’re built once here, but the code still base64-encodes via `Buffer.from(sourceMapString, "utf8")...` inside the `[url]` replacement later. If `append` contains multiple `[url]` placeholders, it will re-encode the same JSON multiple times. Consider precomputing the base64 (and/or UTF-8 Buffer) once alongside `sourceMapString` and reusing it in the replacements (or adjust the comment to match the actual behavior).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
Right — `URL_COMMENT_REGEXP` is a `/g` regex, so a template with multiple `[url]` placeholders would re-encode the same JSON per match. And my "build them once here" comment was carried over from the old code that had a real `sourceMapBuffer` here; it no longer matched what the code did.

Fixed in `7eff261`: precompute `sourceMapBase64` alongside `sourceMapString` and reuse it in the replacement callback. Comment updated to describe the actual behaviour and call out the `/g`-regex motivation.

body: The Node.js capability check is too weak for the way the plugin calls `stripTypeScriptTypes`. Node 22.6 already exposes `module.stripTypeScriptTypes`, but `mode: "transform"` is only supported starting in 22.7; on 22.6 this will likely throw at runtime with a confusing error. Please either (a) check the Node version (>= 22.7) before enabling this path, or (b) detect support for `mode: "transform"` and throw a clear `ModuleBuildError` explaining the minimum required Node version.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Auto-enabling `experiments.typescript` when `experiments.futureDefaults` is true can cause hard build failures on Node versions that don't support `module.stripTypeScriptTypes` (or don't support `mode: "transform"`). Even projects that aren't intentionally using TS could start resolving `.ts` sources from dependencies due to the updated resolve defaults, and then hit the Node capability error at build time. Consider defaulting `experiments.typescript` based on Node support (or leaving it undefined by default and requiring explicit opt-in) to avoid surprising runtime failures when `futureDefaults` is enabled on older Node versions.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The documented limitations here conflict with the actual implementation/tests. The plugin calls `stripTypeScriptTypes` with `mode: "transform"` and the configCases include `enum` and `namespace` coverage, so stating that `enum`/`namespace` are "NOT handled" is misleading. Please update this comment to match the real supported/unsupported syntax (at least keep it consistent with the behavior enforced by TypeScriptPlugin and the configCases).

body: The Node.js version guard only checks for the presence of `module.stripTypeScriptTypes`, but the plugin always calls it with `{ mode: "transform" }`. body:
## Property access on null or undefined The base expression of this property access is always undefined. --- To fix this safely, normalize the imported page value once at the top of the file and use that normalized string for all regex/matching and assertions. This avoids property access on undefined/null while preserving existing behavior when page is already a string.

Best single fix in this file:

  • In test/configCases/html/inline-script/index.js, introduce a local pageContent initialized as:
    • page when it is a string
    • otherwise "" (safe fallback)
  • Replace direct page.matchAll(...) calls with pageContent.matchAll(...).
  • Update expect(...) uses from page to pageContent so tests consistently validate the same normalized value.

No new imports or dependencies are needed.
author: github-code-quality

body:
## Property access on null or undefined The base expression of this property access is always undefined. --- Use a safe string fallback for page at the point where regex matching occurs, so property access never happens on undefined/null.

Best fix in this snippet: introduce a local normalized value (for example const pageContent = typeof page === "string" ? page : "";) and use pageContent.matchAll(...) instead of page.matchAll(...) in the affected test. This preserves behavior when page is valid, avoids runtime exceptions when it is not, and satisfies static analysis.

Change needed in test/configCases/html/inline-script/index.js:

  • In the test "should auto-upgrade classic inline <script> to type=module when output.module is on", add pageContent before moduleTaggedSrcs.
  • Replace both page.matchAll(...) calls in that array with pageContent.matchAll(...).

No imports or external dependencies are required.
author: github-code-quality

body:
## Call to eval-like DOM function Avoid using functions that evaluate strings as code. --- Use a safe DOM API instead of document.write.
Best fix here: replace the document.write("done") call with creation of a text node (or element text) appended to the document body. This keeps visible output (done) while removing use of an eval-like sink.

In test/configCases/html/inline-script/page.html, update the script block near lines 31–34:

  • Remove document.write("done");
  • Add DOM-safe text insertion, e.g.:
    • document.body.appendChild(document.createTextNode("done"));

No imports or external dependencies are required.

body: This new serializable dependency is not added to `lib/util/internalSerializables.js` (unlike `HtmlInlineStyleDependency` and `HtmlScriptSrcDependency`), so filesystem cache deserialization cannot load `webpack/lib/dependencies/HtmlInlineScriptDependency` and cached HTML modules with inline scripts will fail or warn when restored. Please register it there alongside the other HTML dependencies.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This comment is now inconsistent with the behavior under test: the parser drops `type="module"` when `output.module` is off, and the assertions below expect it to be removed rather than kept. Please update the comment so it doesn't document the opposite behavior.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This snapshot key does not correspond to any test in `index.js` (the matching test is now named `should bundle inline <script type=module> as an ES-module chunk`). Leaving the obsolete snapshot in `ConfigTest.snap` will cause the snapshot suite to report an obsolete snapshot instead of passing cleanly.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This checks for a non-empty `src` value instead of the presence of the attribute. An HTML `<script src>` with an empty or valueless `src` still ignores its inline body in the browser, but this code treats it as an inline script and will bundle/execute the body, changing runtime behavior. Use attribute presence (`attrs.has("src")`) for this guard.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Returning a position after `</script>` from the `openTag` callback leaves `walkHtmlTokens` in script-data state (it sets the content mode after the callback returns). As a result, a `<!-- webpackIgnore: true -->` script causes all following markup to be scanned as script text and later URLs are not processed. For rawtext tags the callback should not skip past the close tag unless the walker state is also reset.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: This comment still says this branch skips past `</style>`, but the code now returns `end` and relies on `walkHtmlTokens` to consume the rawtext and close tag. Please update the comment to match the new control flow.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: Using `encodeURIComponent` here sends the content through `decodeDataURI`, which decodes non-base64 data URIs with `Buffer.from(..., "ascii")`. That corrupts non-ASCII inline script source (for example string literals or identifiers containing Unicode). Encode the virtual request as UTF-8 bytes (e.g. base64) so inline scripts round-trip arbitrary JavaScript text correctly.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The `type` reconciliation logic here duplicates the `<script src>` branch above, including the quote handling and whitespace trimming for removal. Since both paths must stay symmetric, extracting a small helper to add/replace/drop the `type` attribute would reduce the risk of the two behaviors drifting again.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: A simple regex does not match the tokenizer's script-data state for all valid script contents. `walkHtmlTokens` implements the escaped/double-escaped script states where some `</script>` substrings are not the closing tag, but this regex still picks the first textual match, so those scripts can be split early and leave the remainder in the HTML. Reuse the tokenizer's close-tag position instead of independently matching with this regex.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body:
## Call to eval-like DOM function Avoid using functions that evaluate strings as code. --- Replace document.write("done") with a safe DOM text update that preserves behavior (showing done in the page) without using eval-like parsing APIs.

Best single change in test/configCases/html/inline-script/page.html:

  • In the inline script near lines 33–36, replace the document.write call with creation of a text node (or equivalent text-safe insertion) and append it to document.body.
  • No new imports, methods, or dependencies are required.

Recommended replacement:

  • document.body.appendChild(document.createTextNode("done"));

This keeps functionality (renders done) while removing the flagged API.

body:
## Pull request overview This PR replaces webpack’s persistent filesystem cache implementation and serialization stack with a new compact binary format (Encoder/Decoder + FileStore) and a segmented on-disk cache strategy (`DiskCacheStrategy` + cache index/segments), updating existing serializable classes and tests accordingly. **Changes:** - Introduce a new binary serialization system (`lib/serialization/*`) with type registration via `TypeRegistry`. - Replace the pack-file filesystem cache with a new disk cache layout (`DiskCacheStrategy`, cache index + immutable segments). - Update core webpack classes to use the new Encoder/Decoder serializer context API and add focused unit tests for the new serializer behavior. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 137 out of 138 changed files in this pull request and generated 3 comments.

body: `restore()` updates `entry.lastAccess` in the in-memory index, but this never marks the index as dirty. When a compilation only reads from the cache (no new entries/build deps), `afterAllStored()` will skip writing `index.bin`, so access times won’t persist and `maxAge` GC can incorrectly evict recently-used entries. Consider tracking a separate dirty flag for access-time updates (or marking `invalid = true` when `lastAccess` changes) and throttling index writes if needed.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: During compaction, `data: oldMap.get(identifier)` can be `undefined` when a segment fails to load cleanly (or the identifier is missing). Persisting `undefined` risks turning valid cache entries into “not handled” reads later (since cache layers treat `undefined` specially). Consider skipping survivors whose data is missing and/or aborting compaction for this segment when the loaded segment map doesn’t contain all live identifiers.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `makeSerializable` accepts an `options` parameter but it’s currently unused. Either wire these options into the codec/TypeRegistry registration (if intended) or remove the parameter/JSDoc to avoid a misleading public helper signature.

## Pull request overview This PR updates webpack’s benchmark harness to integrate CodSpeed’s Tinybench plugin and to support walltime (timing) benchmarks on CodSpeed’s `codspeed-macro` runners, simplifying the benchmark setup and CI workflow. **Changes:** - Switch benchmark instrumentation from a custom `@codspeed/core` integration to `@codspeed/tinybench-plugin`. - Rework `BenchmarkTestCases.benchmark.mjs` to register walltime-friendly scenarios (cold builds, watch rebuilds, and cache warmups) without baseline/sharding logic. - Update the GitHub Actions workflow to run as a single CodSpeed walltime job on `codspeed-macro`. ### Reviewed changes Copilot reviewed 3 out of 4 changed files in this pull request and generated 2 comments. | File | Description | | ---- | ----------- | | `test/BenchmarkTestCases.benchmark.mjs` | Replaces custom CodSpeed harness with `@codspeed/tinybench-plugin`, adds scenario-based task registration, removes sharding/baseline logic. | | `.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml` | Moves benchmarks to `codspeed-macro` runner and switches to walltime mode; simplifies job structure. | | `package.json` | Updates benchmark script flags and swaps dependency from `@codspeed/core` to `@codspeed/tinybench-plugin`. | | `yarn.lock` | Adds lock entries for `@codspeed/tinybench-plugin` and transitive deps (e.g., `stack-trace`). |
Comments suppressed due to low confidence (1) **.github/workflows/benchmarks.yml:44** * The workflow no longer excludes the `*-on-schedule` benchmark cases (previously done via `NEGATIVE_FILTER: on-schedule`). As a result, `test/benchmarkCases/typescript-long-on-schedule` will run on every PR/push, which likely increases runtime substantially. If these cases are still intended to be schedule-only, restore `NEGATIVE_FILTER: on-schedule` (or pass `--negative-filter on-schedule` to `yarn benchmark`). ``` - name: Run benchmarks uses: CodSpeedHQ/action@3194d9a39c4d46684cb44bf7207fc56626aad8fd # v4.15.1 with: run: yarn benchmark mode: "walltime" ```
--- 💡 Add Copilot custom instructions for smarter, more guided reviews. Learn how to get started.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `parseArgs` parses a `--ci` flag and returns `ci`, but the returned `ci` value is never used anywhere in this script. This dead option is confusing for callers (especially since the workflow previously passed `--ci`). Either remove `--ci` support or use it to apply CI-specific defaults (e.g., default negative filter / iterations).
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The `warmFilesystemCache` scenario currently runs an extra full compile in `beforeEach` for every benchmark case (`await runWebpack(config)`), even when the case is not configured with `cache: { type: "filesystem" }`. In this repo, only `test/benchmarkCases/cache-filesystem/webpack.config.mjs` enables filesystem cache, so this scenario will roughly double benchmark runtime for most cases without actually measuring a “warm filesystem cache” effect. Consider only enabling this scenario for cases with `cache.type === "filesystem"`, or conditionally skipping the warm-up run when filesystem cache isn't configured.

body:
```suggestion
Add `CssModulesPlugin.getCompilationHooks(compilation).orderModules` hook. The hook is called once per CSS source type (CSS imports, CSS modules) with the chunk's modules pre-sorted by full module name; taps may return an ordered `Module[]` to override webpack's default import-order topological sort, or return `undefined` to keep the default. This lets a plugin enforce a deterministic CSS module order (e.g. by file path) and side-step the "Conflicting order between css ..." warning.
```

the warning isn't related of the migration..

body: This reuses the JS-string rendering path for the standalone HTML source, but that path resolves `[webpack/auto]` placeholders with an empty undo path. For extracted files emitted into a subdirectory via `output.htmlFilename`, rewritten asset and chunk URLs will still be root-relative strings like `31d6...png`/`main.js`, so the browser resolves them under the HTML subdirectory instead of the output root. The HTML extract needs to render with an undo path computed from the final HTML filename.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: `output.htmlFilename` and `output.htmlChunkFilename` are declared as filename templates, so users can reasonably inherit/use `[fullhash]` from `output.filename`. This call doesn't pass the render manifest `hash`/`fullHash` into `getPathWithInfo`, so a template containing `[fullhash]` or `[hash]` will fail with “Path variable [fullhash] not implemented in this context” during rendering.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: The generated sibling tags only preserve the element kind, dropping attributes from the original tag. If the original `<script>`/`<link rel="modulepreload">` carries attributes such as `nonce` for CSP, `crossorigin`, `integrity`, `referrerpolicy`, `defer`, or `async`, the added runtime/split-chunk tags won't have them and can be blocked or loaded with different semantics than the referenced entry.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

body: For entries chained with `dependOn`, this only skips the parent entry chunk, not the parent/runtime chunk returned by `entrypoint.getRuntimeChunk()`. With `optimization.runtimeChunk`, every dependent HTML script will therefore insert the same parent runtime chunk again even though the leader script tag already loads it, which can duplicate runtime execution and produce extra script tags contrary to the intended de-duplication.
author: copilot-pull-request-reviewer

Other config cases that use `matchAll` add a `test.filter.js` or avoid the API, so this should use a Node-10-compatible extraction or add an appropriate filter.
